clean_body: Our apocalyptic prophecy has come to fruition. MAGA is opening the gates of hell in the 2025 Pennsylvania Supreme Court election and is about to pull off one of the biggest electoral coups in history | | | | | == | Peter, For the last few months, we issued a dire warnings to any Democrats willing to listen: MAGA is opening the gates of hell in the 2025 Pennsylvania Supreme Court election and is weeks away from pulling off one of the biggest electoral coups in American history. We knew this was coming because if MAGA wins and seizes the PA Supreme Court, Pennsylvania is theirs for the taking. Democrats never win Pennsylvania again. 19 electoral college votes in the penultimate swing state--gone forever. These warnings went largely unheeded... now our apocalyptic prophecy has come to fruition. An unprecedented surprise attack by MAGA is in full swing and democrats have been caught completely off guard. We are hanging on for dear life. | == | | | | Right now, Defend Our Courts is one of the only democratic organizations organizing the defense of PA's democratic Supreme Court justices and we are facing nothing short of a tidal wave of MAGA spending. Thankfully, enough small dollar democratic grassroots donors listened to our warning and we are now a serious player in this race having raised $1.5 million. It's a start, but if we want to continue putting up a fight as MAGA spending climbs into the tens and soon hundreds of millions our fundraising needs to go supersonic. We have 32 days to turn things around or it's game over for Democrats ever winning a Presidential election again. This is our last stand. == | | == | | == | | | | == This is MAGA's endgame, everything is on the line here. We have the momentum. We have the story on our side. **But momentum without action doesn’t win elections.**
Remember, this race isn’t just about one seat — it’s about control of the House of Delegates, protecting our progress, and keeping Virginia moving forward.

clean_body: | | | Did you see the news? The Prince William Times is clear: our race could decide the balance of power in the Virginia House of Delegates. Here’s the truth — this election will come down to turnout. Last year, Kamala Harris carried this district by less than 1%. That razor-thin margin shows what’s possible — but only if we turn out every single Democratic voter in this Prince William County district. That means no one can sit this one out. Not one supporter.Not one family. Not one block. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. Our opponents know this, too, and they’re pouring resources into turning out their voters. The only way we flip this seat back to Blue is if we have the resources to: Knock on every door Call every supporter Get every last voter to the polls In this fight, every dollar matters. Every voter matters. We have the momentum. We have the story on our side. But momentum without action doesn’t win elections. Remember, this race isn’t just about one seat — it’s about control of the House of Delegates, protecting our progress, and keeping Virginia moving forward. So, with your help, let’s make sure this seat goes Blue — and that Virginia’s House of Delegates remains in hands that protect our values. Thank you for standing with me, Elizabeth Guzman | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District. | | | | **Join us for Take a Hike for Loneliness!** Let's come together and tackle loneliness while enjoying the great outdoors at **Sawyer Camp Trail**. Lace up your hiking boots and get ready for a day filled with scenic views, fresh air, and good company. Whether you're a seasoned hiker or just looking for a fun way to connect with others, this event is perfect for you. Don't miss out on this opportunity to make new friends and explore nature.

If you have already RSVPed, thank you! Please remember to bring a water bottle!

name: New PA Project Education Fund
subject: 32 days until Election Day!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
body:
<a clicktracking=off href="http://example.com">
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/NPP_Ed_logo_RGB_green1-01.jpg]
Hi Friend,
Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter!
Earlier this week, the PA [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] House State Government Committee [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house.
An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills.
HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.”
State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [https://www.politicspa.com/state-government-committee-sends-voter-id-election-omnibus-bills-to-house-floor/141399] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.”
HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.”
Among other changes, [https://penncapital-star.com/campaigns-elections/voter-id-and-vote-by-mail-reform-bills-move-to-pa-house] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers.
The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed.
The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error.

This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [https://www.democracydocket.com/news-alerts/a-double-win-for-voters-in-pennsylvania] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls.
First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/445-2025-04-24-Order-denying-PA-Attorney-Generals-motion-to-intervene.pdf] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots.
The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/cases/pennsylvania-undated-and-wrongly-dated-mail-in-ballots-challenge-eakin/] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted.
After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/441-2025-04-02-Notice-Of-Appeal-.pdf] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary.
Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as Citizen_AG, filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/1-2024-10-29-Complaint.pdf] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act.
The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls.
District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/67-2025-05-02-Memorandum-.pdf] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place.
In law, standing [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standing_(law)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case.
In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money]
The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests.
In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money] .

News You Can Use
There are things every one of us can do [https://www.democracydocket.com/opinion/we-have-more-power-than-trump-wants-us-to-believe-heres-what-you-can-do/] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last.
1. Educate Yourself
Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally.
Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more.
* New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pittsburgh-mayor-election-2025-candidates-quiz]
* Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections/assistant/]
* Everything you need to know about mail voting [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-mail-ballot-how-to-request-fill-out-return] , becoming a poll worker [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide]
* Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pennsylvania-primary-election-day-2025-voter-information-registration]
* Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-commonwealth-superior-court-candidates] , vetting judicial candidates [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-candidate-vetting-guide]
* Guides to State College school board [https://www.spotlightpa.org/statecollege/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-state-college-school-board-anderson-huff-barlow-black-desmarais-mcternan-sogor] , Reading City Council president [https://www.spotlightpa.org/berks/2025/04/reading-city-council-president-primary-election-2025]
* Guides to voting as a college student [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-college-student-voting-guide]
En Español
* Guía para votar por correo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pensilvania-elecciones-primarias-2025-votar-correo-como-llenar] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/reading-concejo-municipal-primarias-elecciones-2025]
* Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-primarias-elecciones-2025-votar-informacion] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-elecciones-2025-como-convertirse-trabajador-electoral]
2. Stay Engaged
To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win.
Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [https://www.inquirer.com/politics/pennsylvania/montgomery-county-commissioner-neil-makhija-emmy-20250508.html] )
3. Share What You Learn
Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting.
Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year!
4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations
Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work.
Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above!
5. Become a Trained Poll Worker
Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process.
Here are some resources that may be helpful:
Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [https://penncapital-star.com/briefs/advocacy-groups-offer-training-for-pa-primary-poll-watchers/]
Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide/]
Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [https://fcfreepresspa.com/poll-workers-needed-for-pennsylvanias-2025-primary-election/]
6. Engage Your Elected Officials
Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise.
7. Vote in Every Election
Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility.
Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th!

clean_body: [] Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter! Earlier this week, the PA [] House State Government Committee [] [] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house. An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills. HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.” State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.” HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.” Among other changes, [] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers. One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls. First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots. The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted. After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary. Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as CitizenAG, filed [] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act. The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls. District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place. In law, standing [)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case. In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [] The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests. In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [] . [] We are hiring! Let's provide civic education together! We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania. Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote! Positions start at $20 an hour. Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org] EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org] News You Can Use There are things every one of us can do [] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last. 1. Educate Yourself Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally. Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more. New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [] Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [] Everything you need to know about mail voting [] , becoming a poll worker [] Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [] Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [] , vetting judicial candidates [] Guides to State College school board [] , Reading City Council president [] Guides to voting as a college student [] En Español Guía para votar por correo [] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [] * Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [] 2. Stay Engaged To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win. Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [] ) 3. Share What You Learn Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting. Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year! 4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work. Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above! 5. Become a Trained Poll Worker Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process. Here are some resources that may be helpful: Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [] Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [] Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [] 6. Engage Your Elected Officials Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise. 7. Vote in Every Election Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility. Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th! Recent Events [] Solidarity for Immigrants On Monday, May 5th, also Cinco de Mayo, over 300 people, representing 18 community organizations from across Pennsylvania, gathered at the State Capitol in Harrisburg to rally for immigrant rights. We amplified voices that have been silenced, advocated against injustice, and showed that solidarity is not just a word—it is a force that creates change. This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division. [] [] [] NPPEF on the Ground [] Last Monday, May 5th was the last day to register to vote for the primary election! Our teams were out in their communities leading up to the voter registration deadline on Monday. We helped folks ensure they have the ability to exercise their right to vote in the primary on Tuesday, May 20th. Our teams are currently getting ready for GOTV - a time of year where we are on the ground connecting with our communities and mobilizing them to vote. It's our favorite time of year to build relationships with those we hope to uplift. Dauphin County [] [] Delaware County [] [] Luzerne County [] [] Super voters vote twice a year, every year ! Thank you for doing your part to stay informed and engaged! [] Last day to register to request a mail ballot: Tuesday, May 13th Primary Election: Tuesday, May 20th Last day to register to vote for the General Election: Monday, October 20th Last day to request mail-in ballot: Tuesday, October 28th General Election: Tuesday, November 4th Important Links: Voter Hotlines: PA Department of State Website [vote.pa.gov] Voter Protection Hotline: 1-866-OUR-VOTE Upcoming elections [] DOS PA Voter Hotline: 1-877-VOTESPA Mail-in and Absentee Ballot [] Spanish/English Hotline: 1-866-VE-Y-VOTA Fact-Checking Election Claims [] Asian Languages Hotline: 1-888-API-VOTE Arabic/English Hotline: 1-844-YALLA-US Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund New PA Project Education Fund PO Box 453 West Chester, PA 19381 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: We need to stand up for the American people.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
#
We need to stand up for the American people.
We need to stand up for our health care system.
And if Republicans want our votes, they need to make sure that we're protecting every American and making sure that we can do what we can to continue to make health care accessible and affordable as we work to make it a human right for all.

name: Politivist Action PAC
subject: Our First Endorsement of the 2026 Cycle: our founder, Cori Bush!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POLITIVIST ACTION PAC
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act]
Hi Peter,
We’re thrilled to kick off this election cycle with a major announcement:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act]
Politivist Action PAC is proud to officially endorse Congresswoman Cori Bush in her campaign for Missouri’s 1st Congressional District! Our first endorsement of the cycle! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act]
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act]
Cori is the very definition of a Politivist—she coined the word, after all! She has never wavered in her fight for peace, justice, and human rights , even when the decks were stacked against her. And we have no questions, no concerns, no hesitations about endorsing her.
Still, the truth is sending Cori back to Congress is only the beginning. If we want to defeat AIPAC’s megadonors and truly transform how politics is done in this country, we need to send a slate of Politivists and leaders who will fight alongside Cori to dismantle systems of oppression and build a government rooted in people power, not corporate dollars.
So we need you to fight alongside us.
Donate today to help power Politivist Action PAC so we can elect Cori, and a powerful group of Politivists to Congress. AIPAC didn’t just come for Cori, they came for us all! [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act]
In solidarity,
Politivist Action PAC
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act] Politivist Action PAC proudly supports grassroots candidates who champion dignity and equality for all. Movement for Freedom is a campaign, founded under Politivist Action PAC, that calls for radically transforming how we build justice, safety, and liberation through love and solidarity. Every single dollar helps us build, train, and fight for the justice we all deserve.
Want to learn more? Visit our websites at PolitivistActionPAC.com [https://www.politivistaction.com/] and Movement4Freedom.com [https://www.movement4freedom.com] .
DONATE TODAY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act]
DONATE MONTHLY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_pap_email-25q4?refcode=x_251003_endorsingcori_act&recurring=1]

Washington, DC
20033

Speaker of the House Mike Johnson **[set the record straight on CNN](https://substack.com/redirect/0b145d80-8dae-498e-9357-3ce65a6518a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** as to why Sen. Chuck Schumer and the Democrats really shut down the government.
"AOC will run against him,... Chuck Schumer has to put up a fight and pretend that he's fighting Republicans!" Johnson told CNN reporter Dana Bash.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/0b145d80-8dae-498e-9357-3ce65a6518a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* * *
# MAGA Scott Turner MOPS THE FLOOR with Chris Cuomo live on his own show: 'Stop crying!'
Trump's HUD Secretary Scott Turner **[fired back at Chris Cuomo](https://substack.com/redirect/569e85ab-872e-4ff4-8e51-cb999c676b81?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** for claiming that the White House published false "political propaganda" on it's website accusing Democrats of shutting down the government to protect healthcare rights for illegal immigrants.
**[He didn't let him get away with that! Watch here: ](https://substack.com/redirect/569e85ab-872e-4ff4-8e51-cb999c676b81?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**

# [RFK Jr's Capitol Hill confession ROCKS congress](https://substack.com/redirect/c49228c2-0007-4e90-b11e-eac326f9857c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/c49228c2-0007-4e90-b11e-eac326f9857c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dear Reader,
Not long ago RFK Jr went toe-to-toe with senate Dems on Capitol Hill... And he was NOT holding back.
What he EXPOSED left the room in stunned silence.

**[An emerging health threat](https://substack.com/redirect/c49228c2-0007-4e90-b11e-eac326f9857c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** that affects over one hundred million Americans. And if you were born on or before January 1st, 1965... then you're most at risk.
Angry Democrats mocked him as a conspiracy theorist...
Just like they did when he warned about the COVID Lab leak, microplastics and corruption in the FDA...
All of which now appear to be _**100% TRUE**_.
Which is exactly why you need to hear RFK Jr's 2025 warning _**NOW**_.
You can **[get full details here now](https://substack.com/redirect/c49228c2-0007-4e90-b11e-eac326f9857c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** while it's still available online.
[Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/c49228c2-0007-4e90-b11e-eac326f9857c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
P.S. I'll be blunt, if you're over the age of 60 then the RFK Jr warning exposed in this video will make you angry... but it could also save your life. **[Watch it here now](https://substack.com/redirect/c49228c2-0007-4e90-b11e-eac326f9857c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** while it's still available online.
* * *
# Portland ICE facility under siege with "little help from" Portland police
Portland's ICE facility **[has faced nightly violence for more than 100 consecutive nights,](https://substack.com/redirect/29330e1d-1230-4b19-a841-f3d566f4c163?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** with attacks including bottle rockets, rocks shattering windows, lasers targeting officers' eyes, and barricades blocking vehicles, while protesters dressed in black have doxxed at least six employees and followed staff home.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/29330e1d-1230-4b19-a841-f3d566f4c163?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[](https://substack.com/redirect/3895e112-5dcb-4b1c-b176-ab7a522abbe8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Absent local police response** : ICE Director Cammila Wamsley said Portland police have been slow to respond or don't respond at all due to directives from the mayor and city council, leaving federal staff attacked outside the building while ICE officers inside lack jurisdiction to intervene without a federal law nexus.
* **Escalating violence concerns** : Wamsley warned that crowds can swell from 50 to 1,000 people in 30 minutes, and with only about 20 officers sometimes on duty, the facility would be unable to defend against such a show of force, creating dangerous conditions for federal employees trying to enforce immigration laws.
* **Federal response and investigation** : Federal agencies including HSI, FBI, ATF, DEA, and IRS are targeting Antifa-linked groups blamed for organizing the unrest, while President Trump announced plans to deploy 200 National Guard troops to Portland, following a September 24 Dallas ICE attack that killed two detainees with shell casings bearing "ANTI-ICE" messages.

* * *
# MAGA activist pepper sprays attacker after alleged assault in Washington D.C.
Conservative activist Cam Higby pepper-sprayed a woman at Washington D.C.'s Union Station **[after she lunged at him](https://substack.com/redirect/04f79ef6-0645-4879-9e06-bae9f83df251?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , pushed him in his chair, took his MAGA hat and hit him with it, with video capturing the confrontation that occurred after a University of Maryland debate event.
**Watch:**
[](https://substack.com/redirect/3fc2c582-fab6-4d06-8c0d-1034e1a05c73?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Woman arrested after resisting officers** : After being pepper-sprayed, the woman became combative with U.S. Park Police who arrived on scene, allegedly resisting arrest and assaulting officers, requiring four federal police officers to restrain her before loading her into a patrol car, with Higby and his colleague listed as victims in police reports.
* **Pattern of attacks** : Higby cited previous violence he's faced, including a June 14 incident in Seattle where he was allegedly attacked by three men at an anti-ICE rally who tried to choke and beat him, leaving him with a concussion and requiring emergency treatment, leading him to carry pepper spray at all times.
* **" Fearless Tour" mission inspired by Charlie Kirk**: Higby's campus tour, inspired by late Turning Point USA founder Charlie Kirk, involves setting up debate tables with signs reading "The left is violent! Let's Debate!" to promote open political dialogue, though he defends his pepper spray use as reasonable self-defense after multiple verbal warnings to stop touching him.

name: Vikram Mansharamani
subject: Don't Miss Vikram's latest thoughts!
body:
Read my current insights and analyses

| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us4.campaign-archive.com/?e=93b2c2b824&u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=52876ac164)
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=3d1e4d6c61&e=93b2c2b824)
| | | | | |
# Mansharamani Musings
---
| | September’s assassination of Charlie Kirk was a major moment in American history. I usually insist on waiting for some period of time before commenting on big developments, to gain additional perspective...but in this case, I couldn't help myself and posted my thoughts within 24 hours. I received more (mostly positive) feedback to “[Speech Isn’t Violence; Violence Isn’t Speech](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=1fcaa4266a&e=93b2c2b824)” than I have for any other piece this year. If you haven’t read it, I’d encourage you to do so and would appreciate your feedback.
I also commented on developments at Cracker Barrel, President Trump’s visit to the United Nations, and our seemingly inevitable trend towards increasing polarization. Please note that I’ve reintroduced reader polls in some of my pieces, so please be sure to share to vote and see the results!
[_B_](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=77a2bf4e83&e=93b2c2b824) _est,**
Vikram**_

---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=f8a1eedd43&e=93b2c2b824)
| | SEPTEMBER 27, 2025

Two years ago I wrote an article entitled, [“Global Multilateralism is Dead.”](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=1f6814a17e&e=93b2c2b824) The piece generated a lot of feedback, most of which was negative. Former colleagues from academia reached out to suggest my views were not helpful and global organizations stopped inviting me to speak at their gatherings. But the years since I penned the piece have delivered plenty of evidence that the arc of history is bending away from global multilateralism...
---
| [CLICK HERE TO CONTINUE READING](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=e6688096fa&e=93b2c2b824)
---
|
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=f0c8f20539&e=93b2c2b824)
| | SEPTEMBER 20, 2025

September 11, 2001 was a horrible day in US history when thousands of innocent Americans lost their lives as the result of brutal terrorist attacks. But from this tragedy emerged unity. On September 12, Americans put aside differences, came together, and stood united in the face of adversity. If there was a single moment that captured this unity, it was when, after a joint press conference on the steps of the U.S. Capitol, Republican and Democrat members of Congress spontaneously started to [sing](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=52b076abe0&e=93b2c2b824), “God Bless America”.
Such unity is, at best, a distant memory. In the twenty plus years since, our country has been engaged in conflicts that don’t seem to end, a mortgage crisis, a pandemic, record inflation, spasms of political violence, exploding polarization, and now, political assassinations...
---
| [CLICK HERE TO CONTINUE READING](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=862292f41b&e=93b2c2b824)
---
|
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=d947e2ba50&e=93b2c2b824)
| | SEPTEMBER 11, 2025

The news that Charlie Kirk, a young conservative activist, [was shot and killed](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=2024eba907&e=93b2c2b824) while speaking at an event in Utah was shocking. This tragedy is the latest in a rising tide of political violence that has swept the United States.
In just the past year, in addition to Kirk’s murder, [a man shot and killed](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=e665b759c6&e=93b2c2b824) a Minnesota legislator and her husband and wounded another legislator and his wife; [someone broke into the Pennsylvania’s governor’s mansion and set in on fire](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=914252eb00&e=93b2c2b824), forcing Governor Josh Shapiro and his family to flee; and then former president and presidential candidate Donald Trump survived not [one](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=ca19a243ec&e=93b2c2b824) but [two](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=12ec706f82&e=93b2c2b824) assassination attempts. Violent political protests have also become a regular feature of American politics, often involving destruction of property, hospitalizations, and clashes between opposing sides...
---
| [CLICK HERE TO CONTINUE READING](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=f046f459a6&e=93b2c2b824)
---
|
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=69198f83d9&e=93b2c2b824)
| | SEPTEMBER 7, 2025

I have been a regular and public opponent of [DEI](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=a9b557d565&e=93b2c2b824), [ESG](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=62b3684c03&e=93b2c2b824), and anything [else](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=a0b4607a12&e=93b2c2b824) that leads to a misdirection or hijacking of corporate purpose. All these efforts, I believed, are part of the “Woke” movement that prioritized equality of outcomes over equality of opportunity, and demanded that capitalists be deprioritized, if not punished.
After the Bud Light fiasco and resulting backlash a couple of years ago, it was clear that the Woke fever (something I had likened to a mania and popular delusions I had studied during [my research into financial bubbles](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=3e19024647&e=93b2c2b824)) had broken. The mania, which reminded me of the Salem Witch Trials and the Tulip Bubble, seemed to have reached its limits...
---
| [CLICK HERE TO CONTINUE READING](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=ef5d237175&e=93b2c2b824)
---
|
---
|
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=808b48bfaf&e=93b2c2b824)
| |
# NAVIGATING UNCERTAINTY
---
| | As many of you are aware, I’ve returned to writing my weekly newsletter and have been pleased by the rapidly growing interest in my thoughts. As I have in the past, I’m addressing a wide range of topics, ranging from geopolitics and economics to social trends to technology. The overall theme of my work is focused on “navigating uncertainty” and how a generalist approach can help us all make sense of what seems like chaos.
If you have not already done so, please consider subscribing! (**It’s Free**!)
---
| [Subscribe to “Navigating Uncertainty” by Vikram Mansharamani](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=26a7c0a0d7&e=93b2c2b824)
---
|

# About Vikram
---
[|
---](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=355b829c94&e=93b2c2b824)
| | **VIKRAM MANSHARAMANI** is an entrepreneur, consultant, scholar, neighbor, husband, father, volunteer, and professional generalist who thinks in multiple-dimensions and looks beyond the short-term. Self-taught to think around corners and connect original dots, he spends his time speaking with global leaders in business, government, academia, and journalism. **He’s currently the Chairman and CEO of Goodwell Foods, a manufacturer of private label frozen pizza**. _LinkedIn_ has twice listed him as its #1 Top Voice in Money & Finance, and _Worth_ profiled him as one of the 100 Most Powerful People in Global Finance. Vikram earned a PhD From MIT, has taught at Yale and Harvard, and is the author of three books, [ _**The Making of a Generalist: An Independent Thinker Finds Unconventional Success in an Uncertain World**_](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=50ddd69116&e=93b2c2b824) , [ _**Think for Yourself: Restoring Common Sense in an Age of Experts and Artificial Intelligence**_](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=4546f7b5a3&e=93b2c2b824) and [ _**Boombustology: Spotting Financial Bubbles Before They Burst**_](https://mansharamani.us4.list-manage.com/track/click?u=caf139d4582deef486da11685&id=533826d0f4&e=93b2c2b824). Vikram lives in Lincoln, New Hampshire with his wife and two children, where they can usually be found hiking or skiing.
---

I usually insist on waiting for some period of time before commenting on big developments, to gain additional perspective...but in this case, I couldn't help myself and posted my thoughts within 24 hours. I received more (mostly positive) feedback to “” than I have for any other piece this year. If you haven’t read it, I’d encourage you to do so and would appreciate your feedback. I also commented on developments at Cracker Barrel, President Trump’s visit to the United Nations, and our seemingly inevitable trend towards increasing polarization. name: Eric Sorensen
subject: Thanks to you in your own way
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116505694/573649765/795475316?refcode=em-header&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS85MzkwNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzOThhYjNkYy02NWEwLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GKn-dVKn260F3iHbq-NkSGjkDByxArNkPPYTVLGkthU=)
Peter, First off, I want to thank each and every one of you who supported my campaign through our last deadline. Thanks to each of you in your own way, we cleared our third-quarter fundraising goal. I’m heartened to know that grassroots supporters like you have consistently come through when we need it most. I know you get dozens (if not hundreds!) of emails asking for support, and I want you to know that your investment continues to allow a trusted, local voice to go to bat for us. That’s what makes meeting our quarterly fundraising goals even more critical. Basically, without your backing, we wouldn’t be here, with the privilege of fighting for you. And thanks to you, we know we have the grassroots energy to hold IL-17 and help allies flip Congress – something painfully clear that we need to do. The GOP is willingly hurting the American people today and we have to push back against Donald Trump in Illinois and around the country. I’ll never back down and I am glad you’re with me in this fight. Take care, Peter. We will push through this and win. -Eric

name: Mark Warner
subject: “unbeatable”
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116509564/573656167/1082117603?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_CloseCalls_251003_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5Y2Y5YTUzZC02OWEwLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=97HgK8R8aWzptywoMmE6jusN-EeuFSshSTYHRRo6qJ8=) _We understand that many_ _Americans_ _are directly impacted by the government shutdown. If you would like to pause emails from Team Warner until the federal government reopens,[_**please let us know by completing this form**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116509565/573656168/222818858?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_CloseCalls_251003_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5Y2Y5YTUzZC02OWEwLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=97HgK8R8aWzptywoMmE6jusN-EeuFSshSTYHRRo6qJ8=&emci=2ffc4cf8-5992-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=9cf9a53d-69a0-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=4568237)._ I remember Election Night 2014 like it was yesterday. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116509566/573656169/1082117603?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_CloseCalls_251003_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5Y2Y5YTUzZC02OWEwLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=97HgK8R8aWzptywoMmE6jusN-EeuFSshSTYHRRo6qJ8=) The party of supporters gathered to watch the votes roll in together. My hardworking staff tracking every precinct. Holding my wife Lisa’s hand as the results showed a much closer outcome than anticipated.

Before election night, everyone assumed our campaign had it in the bag. The polls were on our side. Some news outlets had even called this campaign “unbeatable.”

And then we came less than 20,000 votes away from losing. It took three days to learn the final results. I won by just 0.8% of the vote.

I tell you this story because I know we can’t take anything for granted. **And with so much on the line next year, we can’t sit back and hope for the best.**

Not when national Republicans are eyeing our seat as a chance to grow their Senate majority.

Not when Donald Trump is building a $1 billion warchest for the midterms.

Not when billionaires have proved their willingness to spend whatever it takes to defeat Democrats and buy a favor from the Trump administration.

So I’ve got to ask you, Derek – one of my most engaged and informed supporters – to help us fight back and hold the line next year.

clean_body: | | | = We understand that many Americans are directly impacted by the government shutdown. If you would like to pause emails from Team Warner until the federal government reopens,. I remember Election Night 2014 like it was yesterday. = The party of supporters gathered to watch the votes roll in together. My hardworking staff tracking every precinct. Holding my wife Lisa’s hand as the results showed a much closer outcome than anticipated. Before election night, everyone assumed our campaign had it in the bag. The polls were on our side. Some news outlets had even called this campaign “unbeatable.” And then we came less than 20,000 votes away from losing. It took three days to learn the final results. I won by just 0.8% of the vote. I tell you this story because I know we can’t take anything for granted. And with so much on the line next year, we can’t sit back and hope for the best. Not when national Republicans are eyeing our seat as a chance to grow their Senate majority. Not when Donald Trump is building a $1 billion warchest for the midterms. Not when billionaires have proved their willingness to spend whatever it takes to defeat Democrats and buy a favor from the Trump administration. So I’ve got to ask you, Derek – one of my most engaged and informed supporters – to help us fight back and hold the line next year. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately: = = = = = = Thank you, Mark **= | | Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | Click here to . | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

name: Team WFP
subject: Republicans want to make an already-broken health care system worse
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
| | | Peter, On Tuesday, Trump and Congressional Republicans shut down the government, all because they refuse to pass a funding bill that lowers health care costs for families. And like clockwork, the propaganda machine has been out in full-force. Trump is desperately trying to blame the shutdown on Democrats — even though Republicans control the White House, Senate, and House. **But we know the truth, and it’s a simple one:** at a time when our health care system is already collapsing, the GOP is hellbent on pushing through a funding package that would **double or even triple premiums for millions of families.** 1 We need to ensure that Senate Democrats continue to hold firm against this violent, dangerous funding proposal. And pressure Republicans to put their constituents before the whims of the tyrants in the White House. [Please add your name today, and urge your representatives to fight for a funding bill that lowers health care costs, rather than throw millions of working families off their insurance.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRImmimh3jp2d-3TZzmeeGfu-UTQdsDuya64KpuO8JCNfhk4J1UGnmKaBjTbYalOGX1shVeE-X3pK48zcR_awgcd0ju0kuAWdIHOOPnRn3U-YkMjbWbusYNFypW3y_lR6byoRBn-pwLPI8hImcG035sUzhkQkJxTno7-0T-q_z3FKfNv3ix-PGsV9OzTvmK9xZcE5uHvmI3OTXnFjjEb8lq_4UfZV4ivCZTplxJ3P52R_xEGoTNJCmz4rExLOTqsRMscNo9tnIGuu_9_gPXOFPC2lsmNf_DeRsskTiq2SZy4pJ7z6tyaxWczaTgUcT1T_gblbkn_NlTs67RD0FVTwEDGPiLDYoWaZCdJZQmr-qXX_hnMHMUg3cSsgvjkRVuirPwXC5yY5zrZZXC2WJyyEWBVj-Lwoz9BUQGKtaLyVZKMRRAmdOwufLvZmBhCRpA5Vpc/4kf/GFGHPWcBQeqN167VzOfQOg/h0/h001.-KThu9ILE78h__nRFi_qf4ehgNf9RiQE5WJ62X6-xDk) **[Add your name »](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRImmimh3jp2d-3TZzmeeGfu-UTQdsDuya64KpuO8JCNfhk4J1UGnmKaBjTbYalOGX1shVeE-X3pK48zcR_awgcd0ju0kuAWdIHOOPnRn3U-YkMjbWbusYNFypW3y_lR6byoRBn-pwLPI8hImcG035sUzhkQkJxTno7-0T-q_z3FKfNv3ix-PGsV9OzTvmK9xZcE5uHvmI3OTXnFjjEb8lq_X8KCCGoR3bv0qhKMgWXVPAJk2BDg2BEp2JQuamyu5bnim_9TkeLYVQT5OwsHTEK5_ZW0QIcD7qyDIAtNVHTtO5fgu9b3B7NbXFDd4bYE4T0wvWRPgzVmoJapalF9w8XZBZIjquVB4AYGkJgcFh1iwpzqcfNQZQqPmPBMgGw1hakMoaD7XXTNJON79L8LjzedStEUsiwjr4Fsjump8TQ70uz3WOz-pR-BFrxCjLUsTPU/4kf/GFGHPWcBQeqN167VzOfQOg/h1/h001.EzcLflqyS1-mQPRc041sGOElehmlpsZB_wy6IZIRQB4)** Thousands of you showed up for our mass call last night to apply pressure on Congress in the wake of this shutdown. And for that, we thank you. But this is no time to take our foot off the gas. Trump and his MAGA cronies _know_ that their ideas are historically unpopular. They know that their policies have made it nearly impossible for millions of working families to make ends meet. But it’s too late for them to undo the damage, so instead they’re going to keep looting, keep pillaging, and hope that they can lie and deceive their way out of taking the blame. **We can’t let them succeed. Nor can we let Democrats cave to these demands — not when the livelihoods of _millions_ are at stake.** [Sign our petition today and urge your elected officials to BLOCK this disastrous funding resolution. It’s truly a matter of life and death for so many working families.](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoe1DuqQbjt4KoloTZxD-XRImmimh3jp2d-3TZzmeeGfu-UTQdsDuya64KpuO8JCNfhk4J1UGnmKaBjTbYalOGX1shVeE-X3pK48zcR_awgcd0ju0kuAWdIHOOPnRn3U-YkMjbWbusYNFypW3y_lR6byoRBn-pwLPI8hImcG035sUzhkQkJxTno7-0T-q_z3FKfNv3ix-PGsV9OzTvmK9xZcE5uHvmI3OTXnFjjEb8lq_exhMY7Dkc3_XlKsp7qBHIcGfPXD24qEDzbcTOLOCesXGzMvhY6R6SQsPM0uzzzYGS4-R6r6W169bBCOb9-4onf1LxHfeinNUkV_Hkj3mGD8e-PbHGIuAi3qX3q5Pt01Ly_HI-JFJKamwzj9FN2yodt7GFCqYBhhmDNMWhQyKTxkkDNawOfTVrxCg57lBS5UEg4MSlhYGlXYR8mx2b3Lt3swcuy0ctH3FNG-0j9V5ZjU/4kf/GFGHPWcBQeqN167VzOfQOg/h2/h001.CUQCyzM1KUaXpL4OQ0UPWc78vz-t3kyVOMzqMRxQZKQ) In solidarity, Team WFP Source: 1\. clean_body: | | | Peter, On Tuesday, Trump and Congressional Republicans shut down the government, all because they refuse to pass a funding bill that lowers health care costs for families. And like clockwork, the propaganda machine has been out in full-force. Trump is desperately trying to blame the shutdown on Democrats — even though Republicans control the White House, Senate, and House. But we know the truth, and it’s a simple one: at a time when our health care system is already collapsing, the GOP is hellbent on pushing through a funding package that would double or even triple premiums for millions of families. 1 We need to ensure that Senate Democrats continue to hold firm against this violent, dangerous funding proposal. And pressure Republicans to put their constituents before the whims of the tyrants in the White House. Thousands of you showed up for our mass call last night to apply pressure on Congress in the wake of this shutdown. And for that, we thank you. But this is no time to take our foot off the gas. Trump and his MAGA cronies know that their ideas are historically unpopular. They know that their policies have made it nearly impossible for millions of working families to make ends meet. But it’s too late for them to undo the damage, so instead they’re going to keep looting, keep pillaging, and hope that they can lie and deceive their way out of taking the blame. We can’t let them succeed. Nor can we let Democrats cave to these demands — not when the livelihoods of millions are at stake. In solidarity, Team WFP Source: 1\. , The New York Times, September 30, 2025 Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. name: Richard Blumenthal
subject: We will not bow
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blumenthal for Connecticut
body:
Help defeat Trump's shutdown.
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116510032/573658197/623074613?code=jtk1419-su-nd1&refcode=jtk1419-su-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTNhZDg2YWItNjlhMC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=LIglt6rObDLPCFnxRdgvdqBdRkw6MHk058KxOBCsnY4=&emci=32302bb5-af9f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=93ad86ab-69a0-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=9329426)
---
“We must declare that the only way a wannabe king can be king is if we bow, **but bowing down is not an option.** ” – Rev. William Barber II
Just two days ago, the federal government ground to a halt. This shutdown is not procedural – the White House intends to use it as leverage by threatening “irreversible” cuts to programs like Medicaid and other social safety nets as well as mass layoffs of federal workers, many of whom are loyal veterans of our country.
Behind this chaos, there are real consequences. Families are seeing their healthcare premiums triple, seniors are being left without vital support, and federal workers are struggling to pay their bills. Trump is treating these as bargaining chips at a table where he is betting on pain, but to millions of Americans, these are lifelines.

The chaos is everywhere. Inflicted on our nation by an erratic and irrational president and his cronies. It is tariffs – imposed, decreased, increased, at a personal whim. It is the attacks on the First Amendment and the right to free speech in public and on our airwaves. It is American troops sent in to police American cities. It is deploying a politicized, weaponized DOJ to attack individuals who dare to disagree with this administration or its “dear leader.” Meanwhile, he and his chosen few are making sweetheart deals and raking in millions. This president is not a president for all Americans. This president is for himself.
It was also no accident that Trump placed “Trump 2028” hats on the table of the Oval Office during negotiations with Sen. Schumer and Rep. Jeffries – it is an attempt at normalizing his plans for the future. Meanwhile, the Republican members of the House and Senate refused to even talk with their counterparts across the aisle. They bow as supplicants before this administration and agree to its every demand – immoral or illegal. And they expect the rest of us to do the same. Trump wants to control and consolidate the authority of our democratic nation. He wants districts redrawn to secure his power in 2026.
The _Pew Research Center_ recently found that **58% of polled Americans disapprove of the way Donald Trump is handling his job as President** , with only 40% approving. That shows a clear majority of Americans believe that he is failing. Even among the Republicans and independents leaning his way, only 58% support all or most of his policies – down from two-thirds in February.
Trump is facing political headwinds. The theatrics and confusion he causes may rally his base, but they also create openings for real pushback. During these challenging times, I want to hear from you so we can act more effectively. **[Would you take a brief survey to outline your priorities?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116510033/573658199/-983693932?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk1419-su-nd1&refcode=jtk1419-su-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTNhZDg2YWItNjlhMC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=LIglt6rObDLPCFnxRdgvdqBdRkw6MHk058KxOBCsnY4=&emci=32302bb5-af9f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=93ad86ab-69a0-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=9329426)**
| | [Take the Survey](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116510034/573658200/-983693932?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk1419-su-nd1&refcode=jtk1419-su-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTNhZDg2YWItNjlhMC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=LIglt6rObDLPCFnxRdgvdqBdRkw6MHk058KxOBCsnY4=&emci=32302bb5-af9f-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=93ad86ab-69a0-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=9329426)
---
Thank you for standing with us. Remember, we will not bow, and we will fight for a democracy that serves all people.
Yours in service,
Dick
| This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
[To unsubscribe from this email list, please click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/p3FPDNcG3USxiOP5q9s2qQ2?unsubscribedata=l25uIqjRVRL%2F%2Bi7C5aQYKyPVTL2njNRK02TbT75cwKR6j9vboGnA%2BwGIayZsH3F716v2nVLhEfXBXvDeWZtQDU9OnCS7OsDQBiza2StbZcYbX7ckgIPgdq9V0%2FOn7O9Xm159RMnuaXOXU6gUzST%2BRpQDYmbRjZjz648m2I%2FSvPtN%2BNnXv51KqJyRK1XwgTAG7mZ7HOdJBRUcEq15q%2FQWNgUpaN76GBLlTWETVZEPj9TCVkr%2BH1U0S4R0cu91Gxi5MZ1DP27kjPbFKawCZtj8Za8Y6ovOLxm51K8VOrierJ6PTkx282awScDPwTBLzwJzYgFnk4%2FZlKh8Zb%2F0DqzZ3Z9U%2F4KyunGMlMK55JldyS1PXHmkBPXguE3qwVrmA9VeWMeh3Rz3rMM8mlEUE8lypPNK9K%2FlWvxkcHJOvucwDxjKHW4y4Wwb06ZY9gfnkUfP) | | Paid for by Blumenthal for Connecticut

name: Voter Action Project HQ
subject: Advancing in the right direction
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
We must keep the momentum in our fight to move to a popular vote system, even with opponents of voting rights fighting back in these two states and elsewhere across the country. From the NPVIC to other movements to advance the popular vote, we've got to keep advancing in the right direction.
We need to keep pushing forward so we can have our presidential elections come down to the popular vote -- chip in with $25 or more to stand for voting rights, democracy, and so much more >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?refcode=100325_1&amount=25]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
The movement to end the Electoral College is making progress, friend: Minnesota and Maine are just the latest states to join the National Popular Vote Interstate Compact.
If you don't remember -- The National Popular Vote Interstate Compact (NPVIC) is an agreement among a group of U.S. states and the District of Columbia to award all their electoral votes to whichever presidential candidate wins the overall popular vote in the 50 states and the District of Columbia. 17 states plus DC have now joined.
Minnesota brought 10 electoral votes, and Maine 4, now accounting for 209 Electoral College votes of the 270 needed to win the election and totaling 78% of what is needed to effectively make the popular vote the law of the land. Minnesota passed a slate of voting rights protections alongside joining the compact as well, which represents huge progress.
Whether it's through the NPVIC or one of the other legal alternatives, ending the Electoral College forever is a big priority.
We are going to make this happen! Keep the momentum going and put us over the top so we can end the Electoral College forever with $25 or more today! >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?refcode=100325_1&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?express_lane=true&refcode=100325_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?express_lane=true&refcode=100325_1&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?express_lane=true&refcode=100325_1&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?express_lane=true&refcode=100325_1&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?express_lane=true&refcode=100325_1&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?refcode=100325_1&amount=35]
Defenders of voting rights like Cory Booker, AOC, Robert Reich, and Stacey Abrams recognize that the Electoral College is harming our democracy and needs to go. State legislatures in Nevada, Michigan, and more are heeding the call and also considering bills to join the National Popular Vote Interstate Compact.
The Electoral College is the biggest tool that opponents of voting rights have to swing national elections and suppress the will of the people. We have to take away this dangerous mechanism that enabled the January 6th insurrection by any means necessary.
The vote suppressors have proven there’s no length they won’t go to in order to win. RUSH $25 or more to help us move to a national popular vote system once and for all >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-end-electoral-college?refcode=100325_1&amount=25]
Thank you for your commitment,
Voter Action Project's Electoral College Team
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/ [https://www.facebook.com/] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://instagram.com/ [https://instagram.com/] https://www.linkedin.com/ [https://www.linkedin.com/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=100325_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=100325_1&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.

name: The Turning Point USA Family
subject: God is moving
body:
[](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?xtl=1apjtn1vd0mi33btweohehxovxmnj0h6jy82isorkmzo1udyd51ewxwrmr0aegff2us9kbr87cq6q1gnk4t0wdekcz4lccfi3m6x92g2drzro2t33cjnafv&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h) [Mark as Spam](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?xul=t2p76x2ul3l184ws2kiv1kps2pgj0xfnyy9bnmgc7ioqzqssrmrvgheoe9fno868fzwb1w5sotqf8igz50nrkoaljtb6ilo85rpsx0x0ft&__stmp=t3ka7i&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh) | [Unsubscribe](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?xul=t2p76x2ul3l184ws2kiv1kps2pgj0xfnyy9bnmgc7ioqzqssrmrvgheoe9fno868fzwb1w5sotqf8igz50nrkoaljtb6ilo85rpsx0x0ft&__stmp=t3ka7i&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh)
---
Please read the message below from Turning Point USA.
* * *

| | | | [](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
Friend, Even now... it still doesn't feel real that we lost Charlie. His voice, his courage, his conviction — it shaped all of us. And his vision — **to build the most faithful and the most patriotic generation in American history** — is why Turning Point USA exists today. Friend, that's why we have an inspiring update for you... In just the first seven days since Charlie was taken from all of us, Turning Point USA has received more than 54,000 requests from students who want to start or join a chapter:
---
[](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
**God is so good.** ✝️🙏🕊️ To give you perspective, Friend... Coming into this school year, Turning Point USA had roughly 900+ college chapters and 1,200+ high school chapters. It took us years to build those chapters... over a decade in fact... _But now?_ These students who signed up? They weren't asked to... They weren't recruited... **They felt it was their time to stand up for this country and honor Charlie.** Because of the life he lived. The faith he had. The love he showed God, his family, and others. The courage he had to speak truth. And his vision of a better America for us all.
---
[](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
He always believed this day would come. He used to talk about it all the time — how the momentum would shift, how young people would rise up with courage, and how a Turning Point USA chapter would be in every college and high school in America. And now... _It's happening._ Faster than he could have imagined. Bigger than we thought possible. And more beautiful than we can even describe. **Again, Friend, God is so good.** Since we lost Charlie, we've also felt SO MUCH love... Prayers. Emails. Messages. Stories about how Charlie's voice changed lives... or your children's or grandchildren's lives. And so many supporters have asked, _"What can I do?"_ As you read this, our team is working around the clock to follow up with every chapter request. And we're going to need help equipping them with our activism kits. **Every new chapter receives these kits from Turning Point USA with everything they need to get started — including:** ✅ Leadership materials
✅ Pocket Constitutions
✅ Chapter training tools
✅ American flags & patriotic gear Friend, I'm sure you'd believe it when we say it... _this won't be a small task._ But we're going to do it. Whatever it takes. **_For Charlie._** _And here's how YOU can help with this..._ **Every $25 you give today helps stock another activism kit — ready to deploy into a new Turning Point USA student chapter...** How many of these activism kits can you help provide to the wave of new student chapters that are just getting started?
---
[ **1 KIT = $25**](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
[ **2 KITS = $50**](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
[ **10 KITS = $250**](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
[ **100 KITS = $2,500**](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
[ **1,000 KITS = $25,000**](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
[ **Sponsor Activism Kits**](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
Friend, if there was ever a more immediate way you could help make Charlie's vision a reality... **This is it****.**
---
[](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
Also... **Here's one more powerful thing you can do:** 📩 If you have a son, daughter, grandchild, niece, nephew — or any young person in your life — who wants to get involved... who wants to join or start a chapter... **Please send them this link:** **[TPUSAStudents.com/StartaChapter](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?xtl=u3vjmpooycfjt0k83ditfy5u0acxolyg5gnyegh7dbj8yiqinjo8xob3famp86ezshz8it12bycwy5wgxurt0zvzv0okf9lua90ffxvfndk30ovutk7vx0ncjnj5xyum5mvzixu2aapv&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)** Friend, if you've already given... If you've already donated... If you've already been a voice of encouragement and strength in this painful week... If you've prayed, been praying, and continue to pray... **Thank you.** We can never say it enough. If you've read this far, we want you to know this... **You're the reason Charlie's mission and vision will grow for generations to come.** God is so good.
---
**For Charlie,** -The Turning Point USA Family **P.S.** Please know, Friend, if you can't afford to help right now, all we ask for are your prayers. Charlie would have wanted you to pray first — and if that's all you can give today, _we're grateful._ But if you're able to help... if you're overjoyed to hear that students are flocking to Charlie's message and mission... Your gift today helps sustain this wave of student inspiration and gets new student chapters off the ground floor... And ensures Charlie's vision is realized faster than we all thought possible. **Every $25 you give today helps us stock another activism kit ready to deploy to a new student chapter.**
---
[ **Sponsor an Activism Kit**
**Make Your Gift Here**](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
---
Thank you for your help and for your prayers, Friend. **God is so good.** 🙏
---
4940 East Beverly Road
Phoenix, Arizona 85044
---

[Unsubscribe](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?xul=t2p76x2ul3l184ws2kiv1kps2pgj0xfnyy9bnmgc7ioqzqssrmrvgheoe9fno868fzwb1w5sotqf8igz50nrkoaljtb6ilo85rpsx0x0ft&__stmp=t3ka7i&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh) / [Privacy Policy](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?xtl=3wb9abchffceg12dpygr2cxv44hj3ipqqtvyc57tl94mfykdpvvpdpsrczucvw8c426p65k7v2d4wfu12e4lebytwkib1iumyrowkgvdk6gm8lynwxa9353lsxijrq2c3o0ikksvj5yd1g&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h) / [Contact us](http://trk.2025battleground.com/?xtl=6v37a3v8epebu447mifll0p0crtqp7hkfl5fgscfxygpbpd6ksaif1ql991t4znx9x6gkk803hdtcghemacb3r11eg91y8oepvnymtqm2vde86q5kjcvy03jbp9gcutnaf4&eih=kzm8nstj6prmj7bhz7sjvvnysq73bagh&__stmp=t3ka7i&__onlt=h)
