name: Wesley Bell for Congress
subject: Let's level set:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
Cori Bush has announced she's running against Congressman Wesley Bell in the 2026 Democratic primary. Peter,

Today, Cori Bush announced she's running against Congressman Wesley Bell in the 2026 Democratic primary. No doubt in the coming months, we'll be hearing a lot of noise from her campaign.

**But, here are the facts, Peter:**

In a tenure that was plagued by scandal, incompetence, and delusion, Cori continuously put her personal agenda ahead of the needs of her constituents. She voted **against** the Child Tax Credit that would have helped 300,000 Missouri children escape poverty. She voted **no** on the infrastructure law that is creating jobs and rebuilding St. Louis' roads, bridges, and infrastructure.

Currently, Cori is under federal criminal investigation after she was caught paying her husband over $100,000 to be her bodyguard. And she's been accused of routing **hundreds of thousands of taxpayer dollars** into her own bank account.

In 2024, this district chose to go in a new direction. And we shouldn't look back.

[**The people voted Cori Bush out because she let this district down. But Wesley is focused on making life better for his constituents, and he's not done fighting for our values in Washington. If you're with him, chip in to his reelection campaign today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116533913/573726421/-1347839382?refcode=em25_251003_corbus_fndd_gq_act___WBFC_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251003_corbus_fndd_gq_act___WBFC_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=e4923f7d-91a0-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTQ5MjNmN2QtOTFhMC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=eqUira9lvjXrg12vsGn1Y_yTxNFewqOgUHtq6zy6GRI=)

The last thing we need is more elected officials pursuing their personal agendas in Congress. Instead, we need leaders like Wesley. Leaders who are willing to do the work and make the tough decisions that put St. Louisans and the American people first.

Districts like ours are under constant assault by the Trump Administration. That's why Wesley gets up every day and fights for Missouri families.
* He introduced legislation to provide compensation for St. Louis families exposed to contamination from the Manhattan Project.
* He co-sponsored a bill to invest $360 million into local gun buyback programs to make our communities safer.
* He supported legislation to keep foreign interests OUT of our democratic elections.
* And he secured nearly $7 million to increase housing supply and affordability in St. Louis.

Wesley is pushing back against Trump's attacks on free speech, science, and our democratic institutions. He's introducing legislation that brings our hard-earned dollars back to our district to help families thrive, not just survive. He's taking a stand against Republicans who are ruthlessly and heartlessly trying to gut funding for Medicaid and raise the retirement age for Social Security.

[**Wesley is in this fight for us. His first priority will always be to hear and raise the voices of people in our community to get tangible results for our district. If you're with Wesley and want to support the work he's doing in Washington, chip in to his reelection campaign today.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116533914/573726423/-1347839382?refcode=em25_251003_corbus_fndd_gq_act___WBFC_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_251003_corbus_fndd_gq_act___WBFC_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=e4923f7d-91a0-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZTQ5MjNmN2QtOTFhMC1mMDExLThlNjEtNjA0NWJkZWQ4YmE0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=eqUira9lvjXrg12vsGn1Y_yTxNFewqOgUHtq6zy6GRI=)

Thank you,

clean_body: Cori Bush has announced she's running against Congressman Wesley Bell in the 2026 Democratic primary. Peter, Today, Cori Bush announced she's running against Congressman Wesley Bell in the 2026 Democratic primary. No doubt in the coming months, we'll be hearing a lot of noise from her campaign. But, here are the facts, Peter: In a tenure that was plagued by scandal, incompetence, and delusion, Cori continuously put her personal agenda ahead of the needs of her constituents. She voted against the Child Tax Credit that would have helped 300,000 Missouri children escape poverty. She voted no on the infrastructure law that is creating jobs and rebuilding St. Louis' roads, bridges, and infrastructure. Currently, Cori is under federal criminal investigation after she was caught paying her husband over $100,000 to be her bodyguard. And she's been accused of routing hundreds of thousands of taxpayer dollars into her own bank account. In 2024, this district chose to go in a new direction. And we shouldn't look back. = The last thing we need is more elected officials pursuing their personal agendas in Congress. Instead, we need leaders like Wesley. Leaders who are willing to do the work and make the tough decisions that put St. Louisans and the American people first. Districts like ours are under constant assault by the Trump Administration. That's why Wesley gets up every day and fights for Missouri families. He introduced legislation to provide compensation for St. Louis families exposed to contamination from the Manhattan Project. He co-sponsored a bill to invest $360 million into local gun buyback programs to make our communities safer. He supported legislation to keep foreign interests OUT of our democratic elections. And he secured nearly $7 million to increase housing supply and affordability in St. Louis. Wesley is pushing back against Trump's attacks on free speech, science, and our democratic institutions. He's introducing legislation that brings our hard-earned dollars back to our district to help families thrive, not just survive. He's taking a stand against Republicans who are ruthlessly and heartlessly trying to gut funding for Medicaid and raise the retirement age for Social Security. = Thank you, Wesley Bell for Congress | = = PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI PO Box 190669, ST LOUIS, MO 63119

subject: We need to codify reproductive freedom in the Senate

body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=em20251003-1040&t=1001&akid=1046%2E251025%2EX5l8Jz ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=em20251003-1040&t=1002&akid=1046%2E251025%2EX5l8Jz ]Abdul for US Senate

Hi Peter,

If you didn’t know already, I’m a dad and husband. My wife, Sarah, and I
have two amazing daughters, Serene and Emmalee.

[ Abdul ]Abdul sitting with his two daughters, playing with LEGO.

They bring so much joy to our daily lives, but when I think about their
future, it breaks my heart to think about how their rights could depend on
who holds power in Washington.

It’s absurd that MAGA politicians want to control women’s bodies. This
shouldn’t be up for debate.

But because we’ve failed to protect this right in federal law, it’s a
matter of contention nearly every election cycle.

That’s why we need to codify reproductive freedom in the Senate — so no
matter who sits on the Supreme Court or who wins the next election,
reproductive freedom will be secure.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/survey/repro-freedom-petition?source=em20251003-1040&akid=1046%2E251025%2EX5l8Jz ]If you’re with me, add your name here to show your support for

Let's get it done,

Abdul

name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
subject: Thoughts on the Government Shutdown
disclaimer_text: paid for by reversing the $1 trillion in tax cuts for the corporations and ultra-wealthy
body:
|
#
Friend, today marks day 3 of the federal government shutdown. This is yet another clear example that [Washington is broken](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116534074/573726907/1257577277?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YThjZGIyZS05MmEwLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_FPd6IOrjgiMu6qlzsAmP2L6ia7IBu9PsrCbYbhSuSk=&emci=906ba28c-82a0-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=6a8cdb2e-92a0-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=27257697). My husband and I raised 6 kids and spent our careers bringing people together. I find our lack of ability to work together deeply frustrating. It does not need to be this way. Congress can and must come together and pass a budget that lowers costs and helps working people. It is our job. So I am working every day to bring my colleagues – from both parties and both chambers – to the table. __But here’s the reality that needs to be at the core of our budget negotiations:__ things are far too expensive, and it is really hard for working families in our community to get by. The budgets and agendas that Republicans have passed this year have made things worse – historic tax cuts for billionaires, paid for by kicking 15 million people off of health insurance; taking food from hungry kids and seniors; and tariff taxes that are raising the cost of everything, including your food, healthcare, and gas. From the start of these negotiations, [I have made my priorities clear:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/116534075/573726908/2033187907?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2YThjZGIyZS05MmEwLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_FPd6IOrjgiMu6qlzsAmP2L6ia7IBu9PsrCbYbhSuSk=&emci=906ba28c-82a0-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=6a8cdb2e-92a0-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=27257697) our federal budget needs to address the problems working people face and lower their costs. I put forward ideas to do just that: **\- Fix the imminent healthcare cost crisis looming on American families.** We need to restore the $1 trillion that was gutted from Medicaid and restore the Affordable Care Tax Credits, before the insurance premiums on mid-Michigan families shoot up by $700+ next year.
**\- Give working people a $1 trillion tax cut,** paid for by reversing the $1 trillion in tax cuts for the corporations and ultra-wealthy.
**\- Repeal President Trump’s tariff taxes on working families,** which are driving already high costs of food, gas, and goods through the roof, while imperiling the livelihoods of farmers and workers.
**\- Restore grants to local law enforcement** so they can hire more police to keep our communities safe.
**\- Repair trust in our government by rooting out corruption,** releasing the Epstein files, and banning stock trading by members of Congress. I talk to people in mid-Michigan every day who are struggling to get by – taking on multiple jobs, making impossible choices between buying medicine or food, and having to tell their kid they can’t play little league. We need to take active steps to lower costs and help working people. I am going to keep doing everything in my power to deliver some wins, lower costs, pass a bipartisan budget, and get our government back open as soon as possible. Thanks for being with me in this fight. KMR

clean_body: | Friend, today marks day 3 of the federal government shutdown. This is yet another clear example that . My husband and I raised 6 kids and spent our careers bringing people together. I find our lack of ability to work together deeply frustrating. It does not need to be this way. Congress can and must come together and pass a budget that lowers costs and helps working people. It is our job. So I am working every day to bring my colleagues – from both parties and both chambers – to the table. But here’s the reality that needs to be at the core of our budget negotiations: things are far too expensive, and it is really hard for working families in our community to get by. The budgets and agendas that Republicans have passed this year have made things worse – historic tax cuts for billionaires, paid for by kicking 15 million people off of health insurance; taking food from hungry kids and seniors; and tariff taxes that are raising the cost of everything, including your food, healthcare, and gas. From the start of these negotiations, our federal budget needs to address the problems working people face and lower their costs. I put forward ideas to do just that: \- Fix the imminent healthcare cost crisis looming on American families. We need to restore the $1 trillion that was gutted from Medicaid and restore the Affordable Care Tax Credits, before the insurance premiums on mid-Michigan families shoot up by $700+ next year. \- Give working people a $1 trillion tax cut, paid for by reversing the $1 trillion in tax cuts for the corporations and ultra-wealthy. \- Repeal President Trump’s tariff taxes on working families, which are driving already high costs of food, gas, and goods through the roof, while imperiling the livelihoods of farmers and workers. \- Restore grants to local law enforcement so they can hire more police to keep our communities safe. \- Repair trust in our government by rooting out corruption, releasing the Epstein files, and banning stock trading by members of Congress. I talk to people in mid-Michigan every day who are struggling to get by – taking on multiple jobs, making impossible choices between buying medicine or food, and having to tell their kid they can’t play little league. We need to take active steps to lower costs and help working people. I am going to keep doing everything in my power to deliver some wins, lower costs, pass a bipartisan budget, and get our government back open as soon as possible. Thanks for being with me in this fight. KMR | We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Thank you for supporting Kristen McDonald Rivet for Congress! Kristen for Michigan P.O. Box 854 Bay City, MI 48707 United States

name: Deb Haaland
subject: Something I’ll never forget
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
Planned Parenthood Votes New Mexico has endorsed our campaign for governor!
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=4821%2E400544%2EV_6LY2)
I’m proud to share this big news:
**Planned Parenthood Votes New Mexico has endorsed our campaign for governor!** This endorsement truly means the world to me.
During college and through my pregnancy, I relied on both Medicaid and Planned Parenthood.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20251003_2_deadline?amount=5&refcode=em20251003-4821&refcode2=4821_400544_V_6LY2&akid=4821%2E400544%2EV_6LY2)_UNM Graduation Day, 1994. My child, Somáh, was born just four days after I graduated._
The providers at Planned Parenthood were with me every step of the way during some of the hardest moments of my life. Without them, I don't know how I would have gotten the healthcare I needed. I’ve never forgotten that. And I’ve spent my career fighting to make sure New Mexicans have access to that same care, no matter their income, zipcode, or background.
In Congress, I championed reproductive health and freedom, and I’m ready to do the same as our next governor. **I won’t stand by and let Donald Trump and New Mexico Republicans strip away care from New Mexicans.**
But, we still have a long way to go to win this race.
**[If you believe New Mexico deserves a governor who will protect reproductive freedom and the health of all New Mexicans, I need you with me today. Will you donate $5 before our last public fundraising deadline on Monday to power our campaign forward?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20251003_2_deadline?amount=5&refcode=em20251003-4821&refcode2=4821_400544_V_6LY2&akid=4821%2E400544%2EV_6LY2)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20251003_2_deadline?amount=5&refcode=em20251003-4821&refcode2=4821_400544_V_6LY2&akid=4821%2E400544%2EV_6LY2)**
Be fierce,
Deb
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20251003-2&t=2&refcode2=4821_400544_V_6LY2&akid=4821%2E400544%2EV_6LY2)**
[](https://deb4gov.us/text-me)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.debhaaland.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails-dh/?t=3&akid=4821%2E400544%2EV_6LY2)

This endorsement truly means the world to me. During college and through my pregnancy, I relied on both Medicaid and Planned Parenthood. Graduation Day, 1994. My child, Somáh, was born just four days after I graduated. The providers at Planned Parenthood were with me every step of the way during some of the hardest moments of my life. Without them, I don't know how I would have gotten the healthcare I needed. I’ve never forgotten that. And I’ve spent my career fighting to make sure New Mexicans have access to that same care, no matter their income, zipcode, or background. In Congress, I championed reproductive health and freedom, and I’m ready to do the same as our next governor. I won’t stand by and let Donald Trump and New Mexico Republicans strip away care from New Mexicans. But, we still have a long way to go to win this race. Be fierce, Deb

We also partner with surrounding counties - which allow us to invest in additional services like and . The result: one of the safest counties in New Jersey, built on professionalism, community safety, support and fiscal responsibility. name: Tammy Baldwin
subject: I’m committed to making Republicans listen
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
| Wisconsinites’ stories matter.
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=5032%2E35263%2ENq7DaC)
Hi Peter, First off, I want to say thank you for your support during my team’s end-of-quarter push earlier this week. Whether you open and read these emails, fill out our surveys, or even chip in a few bucks here and there, you’re a part of the momentum that will be crucial for the many fights ahead. This moment demonstrates exactly why these fights are so important. **Republicans shut down our government because they’d rather Americans lose their health care than do their jobs and negotiate with Democrats.** It’s shameful that, with control of the White House and Congress, _this_ is where Republicans have led our country. I’ve heard from Wisconsinites across the state who are already facing higher health care costs at the hands of this administration, and now, federal workers will have to worry _even more_ as this shutdown exacerbates the issues so many of us face. Your support ensures that I can continue to meet with Wisconsin families and working people. Their stories and experiences during this time matter, and I’m committed to making Republicans listen. Thank you for standing with me. — Tammy | [DONATE](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/4438?c0=stb-em-oct25.EPCm3S&refcode=AU_STB_CL_GEN_TYE_AC_20251003_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=4&akid=5032%2E35263%2ENq7DaC)

# [Hegseth has no clue what he's doing. Demand he resign or be fired.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Te41e6ed8-8915-4065-8e5b-61a8ef3c8664/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4b7aa855-7489-4015-8a19-5b897a8d9771/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | _([Take action, ](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3a37c056-ed1b-4213-974c-5e9ead0c4990/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)then share with friends to help build support!)_ **Pete Hegseth is a disaster as Secretary of Defense, and he needs to go NOW.** He has no idea what he is doing, and has been making policies based on what sounds "macho" rather than what is based on proven military strategy. And just this week, Hegseth ordered all the top military leaders to rush to meet him so he could lecture them about being fat. It cost a fortune to get them all there, it was incredibly dangerous (our entire military leadership in the same place, announced publicly), and he had absolutely no logical reason for doing it. **Having Hegseth in charge of our defense puts America in danger. He needs to go NOW.** [**> > Add your name, and demand he resign or be removed NOW.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/T6786d24c-ef50-4c0d-a486-01b739c407cb/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

body:
| | | [](http://give.statefreedomcaucus.org/ls/click?upn=u001.SSCAglCEkhp55PDWKhWTjk18vO7wfT-2FWuWGj5qYFNqYx7X5ohQ4dCOx5p85so2pLsoUT_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVBZyx1SQGGYhOdwcjZYtHerIXGXCuOGb8SM83v8R7Ta3vzU4cNEBGkdph0aVtG-2FynO4yhol-2BWg7vVWDnxL3Q8Ns1RLszjVYa2764Xp1p-2Bpaqu6abzIo5-2FA4esQSoiP-2F5ncogNFHAXK-2BgQCGgDl82t7pU2xMDwQC8a8fhCo3zTgNK27SjcrYnTCBuH8GOJdVyre9dWRugGpNdBTEBCV2ZwYm6Ta414CkepNLduNH54tni4AdyeI-2FCiEJBrqxSy1Y-2By0-3D)

Dear Friend, Our state freedom caucuses are supporting President Trump and the House Freedom Caucus in fighting the Democrats and their ridiculous demands over the government shutdown fight, but things are heating up at the state level, especially in Maryland.Let's get into it.
---
|
---
**CAUGHT THE DEMS RED-HANDED** Democrats in Washington have shut down the federal government because they want illegal aliens to receive taxpayer-funded healthcare.At the same time, they are gaslighting the public by denying these intentions and claiming that illegal aliens DON'T currently receive taxpayer-funded healthcare. But the **Maryland Freedom Caucus** has exposed this lie and they have the receipts to prove it. Be sure to click on this tweet so you can see the proof yourself.
---
[](http://give.statefreedomcaucus.org/ls/click?upn=u001.SSCAglCEkhp55PDWKhWTjiwkZmtXDP4u12gaI7rwUjae8XQZaRZzQyAlLxNMAq3UmQ1sgIgw3OXLOw-2FBX3CM4Ds2pcZZbSsW3Yk8MGFEH0E-3DttPg_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVBZyx1SQGGYhOdwcjZYtHerIXGXCuOGb8SM83v8R7Ta3vzU4cNEBGkdph0aVtG-2FynO4yhol-2BWg7vVWDnxL3Q8Ns1RLszjVYa2764Xp1p-2BpaqgLf5plFamX8RzkOeoJGiERX67f8WvuAcIUR53Dmeh5tzVFq30G55ORZUqqhA4GayCr6C6ZYnH0cs7cNYNhGC-2FCRWVdo2PTvESIHvjNteFkMKmEHjUOnkuyViaBqHOA3nj-2Bo0VK3onaqKhkAWXvvIb4-3D)
---
|
---
**ANOTHER "MARYLAND MAN"** You have probably heard the news that an illegal alien was pulling down a $300,000 government-paid salary as the school superintendent in Des Moines, IA, until he was recently caught by ICE.Your very own **Maryland Freedom Caucus** found out that he previously lived in their state so they did some digging. Turns out, he was registered to vote! When the MD Freedom Caucus alerted the public on social media, Elon Musk retweeted it, and the tweet now has over 18 million views.This story isn't over. The MD Freedom Caucus is demanding that the State Board of Elections audit their voter rolls and comply with the federal government, which is insisting that it review the voter database to see just how many illegal aliens are in it...and if they have voted in our elections.
---
---
|
---
**BIGG TIME POLL IN ARIZONA** A new poll in the Arizona governor's race shows that House Freedom Caucus member, Congressman Andy Biggs, is maintaining his commanding lead.This comes after Congressman David Schweikert decided to enter the race. The poll shows that whether it's a head-to-head matchup, or it's a three-way race, Biggs wins decisively.It's worth mentioning that Schweikert was once a House Freedom Caucus member in Congress, but he abandoned the HFC because, according to one news source, he said _"the**Arizona Freedom Caucus** was hurting his political brand."_Voters in Arizona realize the magical opportunity in front of them. We have a solid and seasoned Arizona Freedom Caucus firmly in place, holding the legislature accountable. With a Governor Biggs, Arizona can become the next Florida — a red wall that changes national politics for a long, long time.
---
[](http://give.statefreedomcaucus.org/ls/click?upn=u001.SSCAglCEkhp55PDWKhWTjqKGI2SdDnbVRUUcbCRlqbgG7JkMX6gZE22Vb9IvQUiwBSv-2Bg4DKg-2FkcuAQ7-2BshUfA-3D-3DeVtB_9XnTPLnaEjN9OHWhKYUffDeECIVzR9M-2B5LaO5RH8NVBZyx1SQGGYhOdwcjZYtHerIXGXCuOGb8SM83v8R7Ta3vzU4cNEBGkdph0aVtG-2FynO4yhol-2BWg7vVWDnxL3Q8Ns1RLszjVYa2764Xp1p-2BpaqjSlk6Va2DjjdxDSf-2BSSeYrplW2yFEeytcaTZhL0GiEcVhnf7YtPdeI4X5Zq8zCjzjn9YmqOzHhPdn-2FtopdxtKEHxjQUTZTcnbCjJdu-2BNupyQ8Mp2w-2FTclczLK0-2BNB-2BigyAEf8nuov0LldOOLKj71pM-3D)

Every dollar counts and helps us ramp up pressure and ensure freedom flourishes across the country—especially now. Can we count on your support today?

Together, we’ll turn up the heat—and the momentum.

# [Why Young Americans for Liberty's Libertarian Agenda is NOT Good for Idaho](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=175219086&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc1MjE5MDg2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTk1MjE1NDcsImV4cCI6MTc2MjExMzU0NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yTPsh8zw0ByxHG-OVzKXSWQWIcE_Wiwt0dQfKGDo-hM)
### Never go Full Lolbert
| | [Brian Lenney](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---
| Oct 3
---
| [](https://substack.com/@brianlenney)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=175219086&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc1MjE5MDg2LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1OTUyMTU0NywiZXhwIjoxNzYyMTEzNTQ3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjExNDgyMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.b4fCr6O8Z3VDFJj5RAmV6VDXNIibCnpWCaf7JE-wUSw&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=175219086&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc1MjE5MDg2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTk1MjE1NDcsImV4cCI6MTc2MjExMzU0NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yTPsh8zw0ByxHG-OVzKXSWQWIcE_Wiwt0dQfKGDo-hM&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2114823&post_id=175219086&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc1MjE5MDg2LCJpYXQiOjE3NTk1MjE1NDcsImV4cCI6MTc2MjExMzU0NywiaXNzIjoicHViLTIxMTQ4MjMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.yTPsh8zw0ByxHG-OVzKXSWQWIcE_Wiwt0dQfKGDo-hM)
---
| | [](https://substack..ARhj4SuFyMea7pP-RSNqV9V45kpan_OiQoUdJtaDXwY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/brianlenney/p/why-young-americans-for-libertys?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

There's a group called "Young Americans for Liberty" (YAL) that's been increasingly active in Idaho politics over the past few years.
They ran this ad less than two weeks ago:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d544b45b-d017-4efc-99ee-12c5e749f55f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
I want to tell you about my experience with them, because I fell for it. And I want to make sure other Idahoans understand what's really going on.
### How YAL Operates
Young Americans for Liberty is a national libertarian activist organization that recruits and trains candidates to run for state legislature seats across the country. They focus heavily on college campuses and young conservatives, identifying people who care about freedom and limited government.
Their process is sophisticated:
* Identify potential candidates
* Provide campaign training, messaging, and financial support
* Help them run as Republicans in conservative districts
* Present themselves as champions of "liberty" and "limited government"

The messaging sounds appealing.
Who doesn't want liberty and limited government?
### How I Got Recruited
Before the 2022 elections, I attended a YAL training bootcamp.
Their pitch was compelling: liberty, limited government, constitutional principles, defending freedom. As someone who genuinely cares about those things, I was interested.
They were good at what they did...
The rhetoric about freedom and constitutional rights resonated deeply with me. I got elected in November 2022, and when I arrived for the 2023 legislative session in January, I was "officially" aligned with YAL.
### The 2023 Conference: When the Cracks Started to Form
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5898d30c-385d-4afd-ae8f-4507757cee75?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[Gage Skidmore](https://substack.com/redirect/16749930-f4a6-4519-9c03-969303aa8f1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
After the legislative session in August 2023, I attended a YAL conference.
That's when the cracks started to form.
I walked into that conference expecting to hear about limited government, constitutional principles, and conservative values. What I got instead was something completely different.
_**They were openly, unapologetically libertarian.**_
Not conservative-libertarian. Not "fiscally conservative, socially moderate."
I'm talking capital-L Libertarian ("Lolberts" as some say).
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/28093e4a-4278-4759-ba16-5ee3de599856?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
They weren't shy about it. This wasn't hidden or coded. At their own conference, with their own people, they said the quiet part out loud. So, I remember sitting there, awestruck.
This wasn't what I'd signed up for. This wasn't conservatism.
This was something else entirely.
### Working with YAL in the Legislature
During my time associated with YAL, I watched their operation up close.
_They constantly pushed chaos._ Picked fights that weren't worth having. Created division where collaboration was possible. Pushed their agenda regardless of what was actually good for Idaho.
The worst part?
They didn't listen to Idaho legislators on really... _anything._
My Republican colleagues and I would try to explain Idaho politics, Idaho values, what our constituents actually wanted. But YAL had _THEIR agenda_ for Idaho, and they weren't interested in input from people who actually represented Idaho voters.
They messed up the 2024 election cycle. Got into fights that damaged relationships and hurt our ability to govern effectively. Pushed their ideology over practical governance.
And through it all, I kept thinking back to that conference...
The open embrace of policies that Idaho voters would never support. The disconnect between what they said publicly and what they believed privately.
So in May 2024, I left YAL and told them to delete me from their website. So did almost all of my other Republican colleagues who'd been involved. We'd all seen the same thing. We'd all reached the same conclusion.
Here's the letter we sent them:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e5a9ef56-3987-440f-8690-2ab486ba1622?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
### YAL Didn't Stop
Here's what concerned me most: When nearly all the Idaho legislators left YAL in 2024, the organization didn't pause to reflect. They didn't ask why so many people were walking away.
_**Instead, they immediately recruited 8 fresh candidates to replace us!**_
And that ad they ran less than two weeks ago?
They're still at it, still recruiting, still pushing their agenda in Idaho.
Here's what people need to understand: YAL hasn't explicitly pushed these specific policies in the Idaho legislature yet. But I was at that conference. I heard what they believe. I watched how they operate.
So the question isn't whether they support these policies. They absolutely do - they just ran ads for "Libertarians" to step up and "activate!" sounds like a sleeper cell, doesn't it?
The question is... _why aren 't they being honest with Idaho voters about what they actually believe?_
### The Libertarian Agenda in Plain English
Let me be show you what the Libertarian movement stands for.
_**Yes to ALL the drugs!**_
[Complete legalization of all drugs for recreational use](https://substack.com/redirect/bbd6dd94-502d-43ea-96f6-9f7af3473125?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Not just marijuana. All drugs. The idea that government has no right to tell you what you can put in your body, period. No War on Drugs. This means no restrictions on crack, meth, fentanyl, heroin, or any other substance. Full legalization.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/0a7951e1-b0b4-4f54-ad3c-1d6c20db89d0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_**Yes to open borders!**_
Unrestricted immigration. No border wall. The belief that people should be able to travel and live wherever they want, and that national borders are illegitimate restrictions on freedom. Not "better immigration policy." Libertarians support full-blown open borders:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/47a43ad6-6545-457c-96cb-b950f4894d7e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_**Yes to hookers!**_
Libertarians support full decriminalization of prostitution. The belief that consenting adults should be able to buy and sell sexual services without any government interference. Not regulation but decriminalization.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/bed16cbc-8d70-4751-b127-cb036c464598?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
These aren't my interpretations.
This is all on [the party website](https://substack.com/redirect/e371892f-8d0c-42c1-9593-15387a0f6480?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)!
So instead, they focus on the words that sound good: "Liberty." "Freedom." "Constitutional rights." "Limited government."
They recruit candidates who genuinely care about those principles but may not fully understand the broader libertarian agenda they're being asked to advance.
### This Isn't Conservatism
I'm a conservative.
I believe in limited government, individual responsibility, and freedom. But I also believe in protecting our borders, keeping dangerous drugs out of our communities, and preserving traditional American culture.
Libertarians don't just want small government. They want almost no government. There's a fundamental difference.
Conservatives believe in ordered liberty. Liberty within a framework of responsibility, community standards, and protection of the vulnerable. The libertarian agenda represents something else entirely.
During my time with YAL, I watched them operate in the legislature. Their strategy wasn't about good governance or effective policy. It was about pushing their agenda and _creating chaos when they didn 't get their way (they still do this)._
They picked fights that weren't strategic. They burned bridges that didn't need to be burned. They prioritized ideology over results.
And when we tried to explain how things actually work in Idaho they didn't want to hear it. They had their talking points, their agenda, and their ideology.
Idaho was just a vehicle for their national libertarian movement.
### What Idaho Needs to Know
Idaho is a conservative state.
We believe in strong families, safe communities, secure borders, and the rule of law. We believe in limited government, but we also believe government has legitimate and necessary functions.
We don't want our kids exposed to drugs. We don't want open borders that compromise our security and sovereignty. We don't want the human trafficking and exploitation that comes with legalized prostitution.
These aren't controversial positions in Idaho. They're common sense.
But if Young Americans for Liberty continues to gain influence in our state legislature, we need to understand what that actually means.
### The Choice Ahead
Young Americans for Liberty has every right to promote Libertarian ideology in Idaho. But they need to do it honestly. They need to tell voters what they actually believe. What they say at their conferences.
What they'll actually push for when they have enough power and they **" activate" **their agenda.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d544b45b-d017-4efc-99ee-12c5e749f55f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
The fact that they won't campaign honestly on their actual positions tells you everything you need to know.

How YAL Operates Young Americans for Liberty is a national libertarian activist organization that recruits and trains candidates to run for state legislature seats across the country. subject: Donald Trump just pledged to ERASE this photo showing the horrors of slavery!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
Peter, we're sorry for sending such a graphic picture
| | | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T9e5dfdd8-ce35-4784-8149-a361309775b8/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

But Donald Trump just pledged to ERASE this photo from Museums and National Parks. Hiding the facts of slavery only causes more harm to Black Americans. That's why we CANNOT allow Donald Trump to remove this photo or erase information on slavery from Museums and National Parks to scrub them of "corrosive ideology.” [So we’re asking 500,000 Democrats to stand with us today and split a donation of $10, or whatever amount is right for you, between Blue Amp Action and the Congressional Black Caucus PAC:](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tbaf93e15-7c89-46e0-b04e-98669a7593d5/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T8300dd43-1a31-4879-87ae-0a40e1619d58/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: The Feel Good Flyover
subject: Giving Mom Peace, Uber Friends, and a Braves Surprise
body:
When a skateboarding accident near the University of Texas at Austin left student Joey Romano with a broken wrist, he [called an Uber](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OpDAQRk9jMpBdYH4CB5uQ9aYbogKX6VoZG9keELcf9Vxh4k_6nt7791peyJ7D_hcPMm53S3FxCXRnT6VQWna-OOxHPE7Cjb5WSi4xBZsXkKBrJWvZLhbZP4uurHFEQG1FRg160gCjnKq3UavTqOVge2WVXCeCfpukHga12hG3sWLzeVNStiBByr7pRlSgYRhH3TqrlegkWS4xNf8jh_Im5594UWq2eFTevEs5s2j_CJgFzPd9NyVafD6rgPnERKFkAbPDg_0jYP6I1DbxRanOBR-y9Yk5U9gp1ZTqtAWEVvWdrpKx583ec-4H0cn9QPY_2PLrXpeB7wAAAP__FMeA7w) instead of an ambulance.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMHOnCAURp8Gd7-Bi6AuWExiZjftskuDcsHbQSBKnc7bN9NX-LdfvpycnF-P-WEpUgo_7I7GBz9Xn-eErzNirXjMgS5KYc97QbvinwUPfxAmd87AQX0J_sXl7CzF96waZzwioGzQiF6NCmDgY7MZZ7nrhJea44qLGMe-X6wHGBY9CjGMDZkPTXAugQPnuu0GK0BBPwxKeqcE6zg6qvlof2dKdUMf3_nCo13z3kSz1VpOJm8M7gzuWy7tGml9LjY924T1M8u79Z4i2YpMTkuOzhb8KxjoC5PLB5PT-U62VAqYGOh1KTZ8rvWMDHQlx-Tko8eQHTouf64V1I2Btt7nwuR0kcPcHMaVF8VIp-5Zx8NuKf6XrN-uexn4FwAA__-Oj49K) | |
# Friday, October 3, 2025
---|---
**Good Afternoon!** Sometimes the smallest choices change everything. In this week’s _Feel Good Friday_ , we feature a Texas college student who broke his wrist, called an Uber instead of an ambulance, and [found a friend](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OpDAQRk9jMpBdYH4CB5uQ9aYbogKX6VoZG9keELcf9Vxh4k_6nt7791peyJ7D_hcPMm53S3FxCXRnT6VQWna-OOxHPE7Cjb5WSi4xBZsXkKBrJWvZLhbZP4uurHFEQG1FRg160gCjnKq3UavTqOVge2WVXCeCfpukHga12hG3sWLzeVNStiBByr7pRlSgYRhH3TqrlegkWS4xNf8jh_Im5594UWq2eFTevEs5s2j_CJgFzPd9NyVafD6rgPnERKFkAbPDg_0jYP6I1DbxRanOBR-y9Yk5U9gp1ZTqtAWEVvWdrpKx583ec-4H0cn9QPY_2PLrXpeB7wAAAP__FMeA7w) who never left his side. Seven years later, that ride is still paying dividends. When have you found an unlikely friend in your own life? We’d love to hear. Just hit reply to this email. And if you’d like to hear the full story, don’t miss this week’s _Feel Good Flyover Podcast_ , where host Amy Hess talks with the two friends brought together by that unforgettable ride. [Watch the episode now on YouTube.](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzzFuwyAUgOHTmC3W4xEcPDB0yVApUad2RDg8yKuwiTCJ5dtX7RV6gP-Xvq-Lu3jOvKSrn8nGFF2LxS20rZlao-oSv3hJc5kf5G_0nKjGyrSE1SGgPkg4gHLBc96dFsFGIiQlyMqTHjWigVHcrYkQ0dwAJ_CohmmQWvsxGqUpjOgHwfb3JgEUAgIM_dF4iRpPxmgVg5bdEShwK7X_Lry0O8W8lxfV_lZmke29tcfaqbcOzx2e9_Jsz36iDs8fad9kmt6vn0ZUGx4b58zrcOqOkGbP-a9v_4a_LP4EAAD__yCEbMw) If you or someone you love has ever worried about memory loss or Alzheimer’s, this breakthrough discovery from today's sponsor, [Synaptigen](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMHOnCAURp8Gd7-Bi6AuWExiZjftskuDcsHbQSBKnc7bN9NX-LdfvpycnF-P-WEpUgo_7I7GBz9Xn-eErzNirXjMgS5KYc97QbvinwUPfxAmd87AQX0J_sXl7CzF96waZzwioGzQiF6NCmDgY7MZZ7nrhJea44qLGMe-X6wHGBY9CjGMDZkPTXAugQPnuu0GK0BBPwxKeqcE6zg6qvlof2dKdUMf3_nCo13z3kSz1VpOJm8M7gzuWy7tGml9LjY924T1M8u79Z4i2YpMTkuOzhb8KxjoC5PLB5PT-U62VAqYGOh1KTZ8rvWMDHQlx-Tko8eQHTouf64V1I2Btt7nwuR0kcPcHMaVF8VIp-5Zx8NuKf6XrN-uexn4FwAA__-Oj49K), could completely change the future of brain health.
|
---
## Daughter Gives Mom Long-Overdue Sanctuary
After growing up in shelters, Ana Duarte, 25, has turned years of hardship into a stable life—and used it to gift her mother, Annette, [the bedroom she never had](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2OozAURp8GOkfXFwykcLFNumy7JTL4Gu7KP8h2YPL2o8wrTPt90tHR-fecn4Y9x-2vCaTd5ubq0hzpKp5qpTxvfHLcQgoHmZVeC2WXmaItMwIqIUFAN1vD_j2r1mpHhNS1pOWo7gpxgnu7a9mNw6qksuikdXKBEZYRhl7aXoHBsWX9oUmADgEBhls_GYkKx2lSnbNKNj2Q5Zry7X_iWHdy_p1Oyrc1hdbrvdajNN2fBh8NPg5Kh6fP1eDjSsFEUV75yFyoiJCCuLjuYqcs0hXFQjanFIRx9bOclNcUOG5iT4E8lRKpFEFfq38VPklIOcE03Yc2a3tc7D2XYWx62IJh_-NTfx3y1PgdAAD__0n7iq4). The two women spent much of Ana’s youth in cramped rentals and shelters, with Annette working low-wage jobs after dropping out of school to care for family. Ana graduated from Florida Atlantic University with a degree in social work and now works for a Christian nonprofit, helping people living in poverty—something she knows firsthand. In her first stable apartment in Florida, Ana [gave her mother the master bedroom](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL2O3CAURp8Gd4zg2szYBcVGkbXNbIqNNEplMeZiSPixANtynj6avML233d0dB736a6cd3H5UAGlWcxUTZoiHsVjrZinxe0uLiGFFdWM2xOzyQ6jLhMwEJQzytpJK-fPSTRaGkTAtkHJb2IQAD0bGivbVvT6KrorB_Z8zkbMgANXgs285zD0jZMvGmesBQaMXS9drzgIuPW9aI0WnHQMtaspX34nF6tF48-0Y77MKTRe2lrXQto3AiOB8TiOS9liPMuf8-9rQWC0al1PujuNqRAYeceAExi_q22xFTP93PKaXcFC7ynQh6uW_rRIv6HOKQX6aZF-4I6ZvitN38zr8gtVLvSHoe8poMdSIpbSZKnXw3nvyvVGOrYE5fx_y_rlvLuEfwEAAP__cA-RHg) as a symbol of “peace, dignity, and a fresh beginning.” **✨ Amy Hess interviews Ana on today's[ _Feel Good Flyover Podcast_](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFu6yAUxvGnwVuswyHYeGC4UpThSok6tSMi4YCpsIlsYuS3r9xX6Pp90m_4f93MzcYU53C3E2kfvCk-m5nqmqgUWkyIW5zDlKcX2Se9H7T4JdLsVoOA8sThBMI4G9NuZOO0J0ISDWney0EiKhiaUaMi4XiH4Mj21EnBe-7JgrUgBA2qifrQOIBAQICuPSvLUWKvlBTeSc7OQC6WvLTfOc5lJJ_2vNHSPvPUJD2W8lqZ-MfwyvBaa233_C7vBx3_sdjyHJm4bkxcPsJeeXj8v38qhl1h4iKHtVm0e9WYUly7np0hTDamX7z8ucqm8ScAAP__tuB2Cg) to learn more about her story.**

clean_body: When a skateboarding accident near the University of Texas at Austin left student Joey Romano with a broken wrist, he instead of an ambulance. | | | | | | Friday, October 3, 2025 ---|--- Good Afternoon! Sometimes the smallest choices change everything. In this week’s Feel Good Friday , we feature a Texas college student who broke his wrist, called an Uber instead of an ambulance, and who never left his side. Seven years later, that ride is still paying dividends. When have you found an unlikely friend in your own life? We’d love to hear. Just hit reply to this email. And if you’d like to hear the full story, don’t miss this week’s Feel Good Flyover Podcast , where host Amy Hess talks with the two friends brought together by that unforgettable ride. If you or someone you love has ever worried about memory loss or Alzheimer’s, this breakthrough discovery from today's sponsor, , could completely change the future of brain health. | Daughter Gives Mom Long-Overdue Sanctuary After growing up in shelters, Ana Duarte, 25, has turned years of hardship into a stable life—and used it to gift her mother, Annette, . The two women spent much of Ana’s youth in cramped rentals and shelters, with Annette working low-wage jobs after dropping out of school to care for family. Ana graduated from Florida Atlantic University with a degree in social work and now works for a Christian nonprofit, helping people living in poverty—something she knows firsthand. In her first stable apartment in Florida, Ana as a symbol of “peace, dignity, and a fresh beginning.” ✨ Amy Hess interviews Ana on today's to learn more about her story. Uber Ride Sparks Lifelong Bond When a skateboarding accident near the University of Texas at Austin left student Joey Romano with a broken wrist, he instead of an ambulance. Driver Beni Lukumu not only took him to the emergency room but —signing him in, offering comfort, and refusing payment. Beni, a Congolese immigrant, knew what it was like to be far from family and chose compassion over fares that day. Still grieving his brother’s death, Joey says that simple act helped him reconnect with the world. They’ve stayed friends ever since. 🛹 Don't miss an exclusive interview with Joey and Beni on today's with Amy Hess. Prisoners Give Animals New Life In five Ohio prisons, and care for wild animals through a partnership with the At Marion Correctional Institution, volunteers tend to everything from , nursing them back to health for release into the wild. Though some are serving life sentences, they spend their days feeding baby birds every 15 minutes, tube-feeding rabbits and squirrels, and troubleshooting wildlife illnesses. Between January and August, Marion released 186 animals and hopes to eventually reach 1,000 annually. For the inmates, it’s more than a time-filler—it’s structure, purpose, and a shot at redemption. | Flying together with our sponsor This changes everything we know about Alzheimer's! After building the most powerful atomic microscope in the world... Scientists at the Weinberg College of Chicago were able to look inside of an active, living brain... And were stunned to discover where memory cells are really stored... And how they influence our daily activities. how a person with early signs of Alzheimer's organizes their bedroom compared to one with a healthy brain... The good news is that even if you have a severe case of Alzheimer's... You can now easily and permanently reverse memory loss... With this new mental training program that includes a few simple 3-minute techniques. WARNING: As you don't need to spend a dime on nauseating meds or take supplements of any kind... Big drug companies want the video banned as soon as possible. | ➤ A Michigan high school football player returned to the sidelines just over a week after suffering a fractured spine during a game. He was met with a pregame prayer circle and strong community support as his team won their rivalry matchup. () ➤ Students nationwide are putting down their phones and cracking open books in record numbers under new classroom bans. School libraries report circulation spikes of more than 60%, with teens trading scrolling for mysteries, thrillers, and whodunits. () ➤ A 9-year-old gave his father a lifesaving second chance by donating stem cells to help him battle leukemia—an act doctors say may be the reason this Christmas with his family won’t be his last. () ➤ After ChatGPT picked her winning Powerball numbers, a Virginia grandmother donated the entire $150,000 prize to charity. The donation supported dementia research, a nonprofit farm, and Navy relief programs in honor of her late husband and father. () ➤ Italy’s Lake Como soccer team vowed to donate all proceeds from their Sept. 24 Italian Cup match to help the community recover after torrential rain caused severe flooding, resulting in tens of millions of dollars in property damage across the region. () ➤ After undergoing his 34th surgery, an 8-year-old Atlanta Braves superfan lit up with joy when his favorite player, Spencer Strider, walked into his hospital room, bringing him comfort and inspiration on his road to recovery. () Flying together with our sponsor ➤ Neuropathy? Tingling Feet? This New Home Device Is a Game-Changer for Seniors. Millions of Americans are discovering the breakthrough that’s helping them walk, sleep, and live without foot pain... and it has nothing to do with pills, injections, or expensive treatments. is changing lives in just minutes a day. Originally developed to improve circulation and reduce nerve discomfort, it’s now going viral for its ability to relieve tingling, burning, and stabbing foot pain—fast. Team – this is unbelievable.

After MONTHS of attacking (and defunding!) public education and undermining public libraries, Donald Trump is now getting his very own presidential library!

Trump has never ONCE stood up for the importance of public libraries, but Republicans like Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis will do [_**just about anything**_](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69348?t=9&akid=23466%2E4449941%2E-pKg1B) to appease Donald Trump and his family.

[**They even TOOK LAND from a college to build this monstrosity... _It’s sickening!!!_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69348?t=10&akid=23466%2E4449941%2E-pKg1B)

The hypocrisy speaks for itself, team – but we want to know what local Democrats like you think.

[ **If we have any chance at stopping this, we need to prove that Americans from all 50 states agree. So please, team, let us know what you think before 11:59 PM → ** ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/69348?t=11&akid=23466%2E4449941%2E-pKg1B)

subject: Scarier than any horror movie

body:
Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District.
https://ericswalwell.com [https://ericswalwell.com]
Friend -

Let’s speak honestly - I won’t be a cosigner to this tyranny. The Republican-led shutdown is reckless, unnecessary, and hurts everyday Americans. Instead of working together to fund the government and support our communities, Republicans are holding the process hostage—threatening programs that keep families healthy and safe.

They hold the House, the Senate, and the White House. I don’t accept this BS Republican shutdown.

REJECT THE BS REPUBLICAN SHUTDOWN [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-Oct2025-FR-October?&refcode=E15524-20251001-GovtShutdown-SB-Shutdown&amount=25]

While they delay, families lose vital benefits, veterans go without pay, and small businesses suffer. This is not leadership, it’s dysfunction.

I’m standing up for truth. I’m fighting for accountability, for honest government, and for the values Americans share. If we don’t fight back, we risk letting chaos win.

I need your help. Chip in to help my campaign so I can keep demanding answers, defending democracy, and refusing to cosign Republican extremism.

CHIP IN [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ESCA-14-Email-Oct2025-FR-October?&refcode=E15524-20251001-GovtShutdown-SB-Shutdown&amount=25]

Thank you for standing strong with us. Together, we’ll make sure our voices are heard.

- Eric

Juliana has spent her career fighting for the people of Illinois — first as the chair of a local school council advocating for children and families, then as a state representative championing education and equity. Today, as lieutenant governor, she's standing up to Republicans as the Trump administration continues to target Chicago.
Now, she's ready to take that fight to Washington.
As the first Black woman to serve as lieutenant governor of Illinois, Juliana has broken barriers and opened doors. But she's never forgotten where she came from or who she's fighting for. She knows that now is not the time to fold, but rather to fight for Illinois families, and she has the courage and vision to deliver real results in the Senate.
**This is exactly the kind of leader we need right now** — a candidate who is on the frontlines of the fight against extremism and prioritizes building a future where everyone has the chance to thrive.
Juliana marks EMILYs List's first U.S. Senate endorsement of the 2026 cycle! Winning a Senate seat takes resources, organization, and momentum, so this is where the power of EMILYs List can make all the difference.

clean_body: Let's send another Black woman to the United States Senate. | = Peter, we have exciting news to share: EMILYs List is proud to endorse Juliana Stratton for U.S. Senate in Illinois. = **= | = Juliana has spent her career fighting for the people of Illinois — first as the chair of a local school council advocating for children and families, then as a state representative championing education and equity. Today, as lieutenant governor, she's standing up to Republicans as the Trump administration continues to target Chicago. Now, she's ready to take that fight to Washington. As the first Black woman to serve as lieutenant governor of Illinois, Juliana has broken barriers and opened doors. But she's never forgotten where she came from or who she's fighting for. She knows that now is not the time to fold, but rather to fight for Illinois families, and she has the courage and vision to deliver real results in the Senate. This is exactly the kind of leader we need right now — a candidate who is on the frontlines of the fight against extremism and prioritizes building a future where everyone has the chance to thrive. Juliana marks EMILYs List's first U.S. Senate endorsement of the 2026 cycle! Winning a Senate seat takes resources, organization, and momentum, so this is where the power of EMILYs List can make all the difference. =** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = – the EMILYs List Campaigns team = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email . We'd hate to see you go, but if you want to stop receiving all email, unsubscribe . Have a question? Feel free to drop us a line at mailto:information@emilyslist.org or simply reply to this email. Contributions or gifts to EMILYs List or endorsed candidates are not tax deductible. EMILYs List P.O. Box 96612 Washington, DC 20077 United States | ---|---

name: All On The Line Indiana
subject: Blackmail in broad daylight
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
We need your help to fight back
|
---

Peter,
Indiana Governor Mike Braun just let the truth slip out about Donald Trump's threats towards conservatives to ensure they follow his orders to gerrymander:
**“If we try to drag our feet as a state on it, probably, we’ll have consequences of not working with the Trump administration as tightly as we should.”**
When asked in another interview what “consequences” he was referring to, Braun said:
“That just means I think the Trump administration, for what it’s trying to do,**if you’re not kind of getting involved as well as you can on the political side, you probably are not going to be the first call when it comes to the benefits** of — just like we’re one of 5 states now — as the agriculture department moves its bureaucracy into the interior; we’re one of those 5 states.”
**This is shameful: Braun is warning that his state could face political ramifications and even potentially lose federal funding if they don’t go along with Trump’s redistricting rampage.**
This isn’t governance in a democracy — this is an authoritarian shakedown. It’s blackmail in broad daylight. And a dangerous escalation in pressure tactics from the White House. **Peter, voters on both sides of the aisle want to see their leaders stand up to the bully.**
All On The Line is doing everything in our power to push back against this anti-democracy attack. We recently led a training for Indiana volunteers to help them understand how they can get involved and testify against this extreme power grab. And we’re amplifying the work of our affiliates who are launching legal challenges to gerrymandering across the country.
**None of this critical work is possible without grassroots support.[If you believe that we deserve a truly representative democracy, please make a contribution to help keep up this important fight.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/116537270/573737668/2109033857?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_INGov_email_20251003-AOTL-Tier1-INGov_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251003-AOTL-Tier1-INGov_&utm_campaign=INGov&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjU1NmY5OWY4LTliYTAtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGVkOGJhNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=mQnKon0NYLp2lGLKjHDcRYaIGnuR_GKUTJyRZgWF_pE=)**

clean_body: We need your help to fight back | Peter, Indiana Governor Mike Braun just let the truth slip out about Donald Trump's threats towards conservatives to ensure they follow his orders to gerrymander: “If we try to drag our feet as a state on it, probably, we’ll have consequences of not working with the Trump administration as tightly as we should.” When asked in another interview what “consequences” he was referring to, Braun said: “That just means I think the Trump administration, for what it’s trying to do,if you’re not kind of getting involved as well as you can on the political side, you probably are not going to be the first call when it comes to the benefits of — just like we’re one of 5 states now — as the agriculture department moves its bureaucracy into the interior; we’re one of those 5 states.” This is shameful: Braun is warning that his state could face political ramifications and even potentially lose federal funding if they don’t go along with Trump’s redistricting rampage. This isn’t governance in a democracy — this is an authoritarian shakedown. It’s blackmail in broad daylight. And a dangerous escalation in pressure tactics from the White House. Peter, voters on both sides of the aisle want to see their leaders stand up to the bully. All On The Line is doing everything in our power to push back against this anti-democracy attack. We recently led a training for Indiana volunteers to help them understand how they can get involved and testify against this extreme power grab. And we’re amplifying the work of our affiliates who are launching legal challenges to gerrymandering across the country. None of this critical work is possible without grassroots support.= If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thanks so much, The All On The Line Team is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. Support our work to end gerrymandering. Text FAIR to 88228 to receive recurring updates from AOTL by automated text message. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. and | = | PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND. Hi null, it's Haley Stevens.
---
I just wanted to make sure you saw our new flash petition about the government shutdown.
It's critical I hear from you – we still need 55 more responses from 18326. **Will you add your name now if you believe the Republicans are to blame for the shutdown?**
**[surveys.signforgood.com/Stevens-Gov-Shutdown/pet](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/6493?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=2&akid=1006%2E49239%2EAmkowI)**
\---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: **Team Haley**
Date: Thu, Oct 2, 2025
Subject: Here's the truth: MAGA Republicans shut down the government
| | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/6496?t=3&akid=1006%2E49239%2EAmkowI)
---
Null, if you agree that Republicans are to blame for the government shutdown, **[add your name to our emergency petition now.](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/6493?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=4&akid=1006%2E49239%2EAmkowI)**
| | [Add Your Name >>](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/6493?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=5&akid=1006%2E49239%2EAmkowI)
---
Here’s how the GOP got us into this mess:
**FIRST:** Donald Trump handed the federal government over to Elon Musk’s DOGE – and they made massive, harmful cuts to essential federal services.
**THEN:** Republicans passed their so-called “Big Beautiful Bill,” kicking millions off of their health care just to hand the richest people in the country a huge tax break.
**NOW:** Trump and the GOP have shut down the government – and are jeopardizing health care for millions more.
That’s why we’re calling on 10,000 people to speak out by midnight and demand accountability. **[Add your name now to our live petition if you agree Republicans are to blame for this dangerous shutdown:](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/6493?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=6&akid=1006%2E49239%2EAmkowI)**

---
Thank you for standing with us,

Team Haley
|
* * *
**A note from Haley Stevens:** null, I’m proud to be a lifelong Michigander and the daughter of small-business owners. That’s why I’ve fought so hard for working families my entire life. Now, I’m running for Michigan’s open Senate seat to continue fighting for the people and communities I love. The path to the Senate majority runs right through Michigan, which means this race is shaping up to be the most competitive and expensive in the entire country. The GOP is already coming after me as a top target for defeat, so I’m counting on your grassroots support to fight back and win. [**If you’re in this fight with me, will you add your name to endorse my campaign?**](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/6495?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=8&akid=1006%2E49239%2EAmkowI) | | | [](https://act.haleyformi.com/go/6496?t=9&akid=1006%2E49239%2EAmkowI)
---
You can also text “JOIN” to 80242 to become a core member of our team and receive exclusive, real-time updates from my campaign.
Thank you,
Haley

Democrats have been in DC – ready and willing to negotiate on a path forward – but the Republicans are nowhere to be found. They thought they could pressure us into bending to their will with threats of mass layoffs and now, pulling funding for critical projects from blue states. Not going to happen. Donald Trump has done what no president ever should — attacking political opposition by weaponizing the Department of Justice to indict former FBI director James Comey.
Federal prosecutors even put in writing that the case was too weak to take to trial — because Comey was just doing his job. Those prosecutors got fired and replaced with a wildly unqualified Trump loyalist.
**The message is clear** : If you oppose Donald Trump and refuse to submit to his authority, _you will be punished_.
We can’t let Donald Trump get away with this, or our democracy is at risk — and no one is safe.

name: Chris Ahuja
subject: Rejecting AIPAC. Building people power.
body:
A turning point for Democrats

| | | | | | | | | | | | | | | [|
---](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=effb861fa1&e=5a6ee82cdb)
| | | | | | Dear Peter,

---
|
---
| | Yesterday’s New York Times spotlighted a growing shift in American politics: **more Democrats are rejecting AIPAC**. I’m glad to see it because for too long, corporate PACs and big-dollar interests have drowned out the voices of ordinary Americans.AIPAC’s push for the unconditional support of Israel — no matter the cost or consequence — is unacceptable. It raises **ethical and legal questions** , undermines accountability, and blocks the urgent need for a foreign policy rooted in human rights and peace.**Our campaign is built on people power, not PAC powe** r. We’re standing up to entrenched interests that spend millions to keep Washington beholden to them — and lifting up the voices of working families who deserve to be heard.**I’m proud to be endorsed by Track AIPAC, and to be running without a dime from AIPAC or any corporate PAC.** With your support, we can push back on entrenched money and power, and ensure that ordinary voices are heard, respected, and represented. [**Join me and donate $25, $50, or whatever feels right today to keep building this people-powered movement?**](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=b5d5c1cc60&e=5a6ee82cdb)
---
| [Donate Now](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=d9613b48e3&e=5a6ee82cdb)
---
| |
**Thank you for standing with me in this fight.****In solidarity,
**[****](https://chrisahuja.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=97f443092fb7c091746070aa0&id=4b4c5c7c08&e=5a6ee82cdb)**Chris Ahuja****Democrat for Congress, CD-32**

---

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Your questions about the shutdown
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
Warren for Senate: https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/370?source=em-header&t=1001&akid=2672%2E4032634%2ESzo5vh

I want to be frank: There are a lot of partisan games and
wild claims being thrown around about the Republican government shutdown —
and there are also real, serious implications for what this shutdown means
for our country’s health care system.

I wanted to take a minute to answer some questions and ask you what you
want to know.

[ https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/ew-em-shutdown25/?source=EMZ27W_GOP_E_20251003_V1S1&t=1006&akid=2672%2E4032634%2ESzo5vh ]Got a question for me about the government shutdown? Do you have a
comment or story you want to share as Democrats fight to pass a budget
that saves health care? Click here to share with me and my team.

[ https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/ew-em-shutdown25/?source=EMZ27W_GOP_E_20251003_V1S1&t=1007&akid=2672%2E4032634%2ESzo5vh ]ASK YOUR QUESTIONS

Here are some answers to the questions you may be asking:

Why did the government shut down?

Republicans needed 60 votes to pass their budget and keep the government
running by October 1 — and so they needed Democratic votes. Democrats have
been trying to negotiate with Republicans to make sure that this budget
addresses the millions of people who are about to lose their health care
coverage or see their health care costs go way up.

That’s what we do in Congress. We negotiate. We fight to make life better
for people. (You would think.)

But Republicans did not negotiate with us. Would-be king Donald Trump
said, “We won’t even talk to you.” They expected us to just rubber-stamp
any bill they wanted to keep their corrupt government running. That wasn’t
going to happen.

Republicans failed to get the Democratic votes they needed to fund their
government before the deadline. Now we are in a shutdown.

What are the Republican health care cuts you’re talking about?

Remember the One Big Beautiful Bill Republicans rammed through in July?
That bill knocked 15 million Americans off Medicaid to give tax cuts to
billionaires and giant corporations. This bill was able to pass even
though zero Democrats voted for it.

These cuts mean that new mamas and babies who need check-ups are going to
lose their care. Folks in nursing homes with Alzheimer’s will be kicked
out onto the street. Rural hospitals will be shutting their doors. This is
already happening: Just last week, a couple of rural health clinics in
Virginia had to close because they couldn’t afford to lose federal
funding.

Democrats are not going to stand by while millions of people lose access
to health care.

And what are the insurance premium increases you’re talking about?

People who buy their plans on the Obamacare market could see their costs
go up — way up.

That’s because Republicans want to cut subsidies that have kept those
insurance costs down for millions of families. If you get your health
coverage through Obamacare, your premiums could more than double.

Put that into perspective: If you’re a family of four making $75,000 a
year, you’re going to pay $3,368 more for your health care plan next year
if Congress passed the Republican budget they proposed.

Republicans are throwing a one-two punch here: Keep the Medicaid cuts from
the Big, Ugly Bill — and also cut the Obamacare insurance subsidies that
keep insurance costs low.

What are Republicans saying?

Republicans are trying to put the blame anywhere but on them. They have no
real response to the health care crisis they caused — so they’re lying
instead.

Their claim that this is about undocumented immigrants is false. They know
that undocumented immigrants are not eligible for the Obamacare or
Medicaid benefits that are being discussed.

They are making false claims and scapegoating vulnerable people to
distract from the fact that they’ve kicked 15 million Americans off of
their health care and that millions of families are going to see their
health care costs get more expensive because of them.

Republicans control the House, the Senate, and the White House. They own
this shutdown and they own this health care crisis.

What are Democrats saying?

There’s a simple way to get out of this shutdown. Republicans can come to
the negotiating table and work with us to keep health care available and
less expensive for people.

Pretty easy.

What happens during a government shutdown?

Government functions are halted during a shutdown. This means our national
parks and museums could close, federal research projects are suspended,
and services like passports and visas are stopped. Many federal workers
could be furloughed and sent home without pay.

Make no mistake: this Trump shutdown will hurt people.

Children across this country could lose access to childcare through Head
Start.

The National Institutes of Health Clinical Center could stop enrolling
patients in clinical trials that could be their only shot at treatment.

Air traffic controller hiring could stop — at a time when we’re already
dealing with a dangerous shortage.

Democrats are ready to get the government back open and working for the
American people. We just need Republicans to get serious with us.

[ https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/ew-em-shutdown25/?source=EMZ27W_GOP_E_20251003_V1S1&t=1008&akid=2672%2E4032634%2ESzo5vh ]What other questions do you have for me about the shutdown, the health
care crisis, or what Democrats are fighting for? Tell me what’s on your
mind.

More from me soon — and thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth

clean_body: Warren for Senate: I want to be frank: There are a lot of partisan games and wild claims being thrown around about the Republican government shutdown — and there are also real, serious implications for what this shutdown means for our country’s health care system. I wanted to take a minute to answer some questions and ask you what you want to know. [ ]Got a question for me about the government shutdown? Do you have a comment or story you want to share as Democrats fight to pass a budget that saves health care? Click here to share with me and my team. [ ]ASK YOUR QUESTIONS Here are some answers to the questions you may be asking: Why did the government shut down? Republicans needed 60 votes to pass their budget and keep the government running by October 1 — and so they needed Democratic votes. Democrats have been trying to negotiate with Republicans to make sure that this budget addresses the millions of people who are about to lose their health care coverage or see their health care costs go way up. That’s what we do in Congress. We negotiate. We fight to make life better for people. (You would think.) But Republicans did not negotiate with us. Would-be king Donald Trump said, “We won’t even talk to you.” They expected us to just rubber-stamp any bill they wanted to keep their corrupt government running. That wasn’t going to happen. Republicans failed to get the Democratic votes they needed to fund their government before the deadline. Now we are in a shutdown. What are the Republican health care cuts you’re talking about? Remember the One Big Beautiful Bill Republicans rammed through in July? That bill knocked 15 million Americans off Medicaid to give tax cuts to billionaires and giant corporations. This bill was able to pass even though zero Democrats voted for it. These cuts mean that new mamas and babies who need check-ups are going to lose their care. Folks in nursing homes with Alzheimer’s will be kicked out onto the street. Rural hospitals will be shutting their doors. This is already happening: Just last week, a couple of rural health clinics in Virginia had to close because they couldn’t afford to lose federal funding. Democrats are not going to stand by while millions of people lose access to health care. And what are the insurance premium increases you’re talking about? People who buy their plans on the Obamacare market could see their costs go up — way up. That’s because Republicans want to cut subsidies that have kept those insurance costs down for millions of families. If you get your health coverage through Obamacare, your premiums could more than double. Put that into perspective: If you’re a family of four making $75,000 a year, you’re going to pay $3,368 more for your health care plan next year if Congress passed the Republican budget they proposed. Republicans are throwing a one-two punch here: Keep the Medicaid cuts from the Big, Ugly Bill — and also cut the Obamacare insurance subsidies that keep insurance costs low. What are Republicans saying? Republicans are trying to put the blame anywhere but on them. They have no real response to the health care crisis they caused — so they’re lying instead. Their claim that this is about undocumented immigrants is false. They know that undocumented immigrants are not eligible for the Obamacare or Medicaid benefits that are being discussed. They are making false claims and scapegoating vulnerable people to distract from the fact that they’ve kicked 15 million Americans off of their health care and that millions of families are going to see their health care costs get more expensive because of them. Republicans control the House, the Senate, and the White House. They own this shutdown and they own this health care crisis. What are Democrats saying? There’s a simple way to get out of this shutdown. Republicans can come to the negotiating table and work with us to keep health care available and less expensive for people. Pretty easy. What happens during a government shutdown? Government functions are halted during a shutdown. This means our national parks and museums could close, federal research projects are suspended, and services like passports and visas are stopped. Many federal workers could be furloughed and sent home without pay. Make no mistake: this Trump shutdown will hurt people. Children across this country could lose access to childcare through Head Start. The National Institutes of Health Clinical Center could stop enrolling patients in clinical trials that could be their only shot at treatment. Air traffic controller hiring could stop — at a time when we’re already dealing with a dangerous shortage. Democrats are ready to get the government back open and working for the American people. We just need Republicans to get serious with us. [ ]What other questions do you have for me about the shutdown, the health care crisis, or what Democrats are fighting for? Tell me what’s on your mind. More from me soon — and thanks for being a part of this, Elizabeth Facebook: Twitter: Instagram: YouTube: Paid for by Warren for Senate All content © 2025 Warren for Senate, All Rights Reserved PO Box 171375, Boston, MA 02117 __________________________________________This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
